from.title: Elijah Widow Oil
from.body: # Elijah and the Widow's Oil ## What happens A Gentile widow at Zarephath shares her last meal with Elijah; her jar of flour and jug of oil do not run out for the duration of the famine. ## Typological significance Multiplication through self-emptying hospitality, given to a Gentile widow — Jesus cites this as proof that grace overflows Israel's borders. Pattern of the eucharistic 'always enough.'…

to.title: Elisha Multiplies Oil
to.body: # Elisha Multiplies the Widow's Oil ## What happens A prophet's widow facing creditors is told by Elisha to gather empty vessels; her small jar of oil fills them all until no vessel remains. ## Typological significance Abundance limited only by the capacity of empty vessels — pattern of grace that fills whatever is offered empty. Anticipates the Spirit's filling at Pentecost. ## Connects to `[[../…

from.title: Revelation 21 New Jerusalem Descending
from.body: # Revelation 21: New Jerusalem Descending ## NT Text — Rev 21:1-22:5 John sees the new heaven and new earth and the New Jerusalem descending from heaven as a bride adorned. God dwells with men. ## OT Antecedents - **Isa 65:17-25** — New heavens and new earth. - **Ezek 40-48** — Vision of restored temple-city. - **Zech 8:3** — I will dwell in Zion. ## Shared structure - New cosmos - City descending…

to.title: New Jerusalem Descending
to.body: # New Jerusalem Descending ## The oracle John sees the holy city, New Jerusalem, coming down out of heaven from God, prepared as a bride; God dwells with humanity, every tear wiped away, no more death, mourning, or pain. ## Date / context Climax of Revelation; Ezekiel's temple vision is a major OT antecedent. ## Fulfillment Awaits consummation; foretasted in the church as the city set on a hill. #…

from.title: 1 Peter 5 Chief Shepherd
from.body: # 1 Peter 5: Chief Shepherd ## NT Text — 1 Pet 5:1-4 Elders shepherd the flock; when the Chief Shepherd appears, they will receive the unfading crown of glory. Davidic-shepherd typology gathered into Christ. ## OT Antecedents - **Ezek 34:23** — My servant David — one shepherd. - **Ps 23** — The LORD is my shepherd. - **Zech 11:7-17** — Two shepherd-staffs. ## Shared structure - Under-shepherds - C…

to.title: Ezekiel 34 Shepherds Of Israel
to.body: # False Shepherds Condemned / God Will Shepherd → Christ the Good Shepherd ## OT Story — Ezekiel 34 Woe to the shepherds of Israel who feed themselves and not the flock. 'I myself will seek out my sheep… I will set up one shepherd, my servant David.' ## NT Fulfillment - John 10:11: I am the good shepherd. - 1 Peter 5:1-4: Chief Shepherd. - Matthew 9:36: Compassion on the crowds — sheep without a s…

from.title: Calling Of Twelve
from.body: # Calling of the Twelve ## NT Text — Mark 3:13-19; Luke 6:12-16 Jesus ascends a mountain and appoints twelve apostles. The number is constitutive: a new Israel is being formed around a new lawgiver. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 49** — Twelve patriarchs. - **Exod 24:4** — Twelve pillars at Sinai. - **Josh 4:1-9** — Twelve stones from the Jordan. ## Shared structure - Twelve representatives - Mountain …

to.title: Twelve Apostles Echo Twelve Tribes
to.body: # Twelve Apostles Echo Twelve Tribes ## NT Text — Matt 19:28; Rev 21:14 Jesus promises the Twelve will sit on twelve thrones judging the twelve tribes; Revelation 21 names the foundations of the New Jerusalem after them. Tribal Israel and apostolic Israel meet in the heavenly city. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 49:28** — Twelve tribes of Israel. - **Exod 28:21** — Twelve stones on the high priest's br…

from.title: 1 Peter 1 Born Anew
from.body: # 1 Peter 1: Born Anew through Living Word ## NT Text — 1 Pet 1:22-25 Born again through the living and abiding word of God — Isaiah's 'grass withers, the word stands forever' cited as charter. ## OT Antecedents - **Isa 40:6-8** — Grass withers, word stands forever. - **Ezek 36:26** — New heart and Spirit. - **Ps 119:25** — Give me life according to your word. ## Shared structure - New birth - Liv…

to.title: 1 Peter 1 Living Hope
to.body: # 1 Peter 1: Living Hope ## NT Text — 1 Pet 1:3-9 Peter blesses God for a living hope through the resurrection of Jesus Christ. The inheritance is imperishable, undefiled, unfading, kept in heaven. ## OT Antecedents - **Num 18:20** — LORD as inheritance. - **Ps 16:5-6** — The LORD is my chosen portion. - **Dan 12:2-3** — Resurrection inheritance. ## Shared structure - New birth - Living hope - Imp…

from.title: Lamb Throughout Scripture
from.body: # Lamb Throughout Scripture ## NT Text — John 1:29; Rev 5 Abel's firstborn, Abraham's substitute, the Passover lamb, the daily tamid, Isaiah's silent lamb, John's 'Lamb of God', Revelation's slain Lamb. The lamb is canon's central animal. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 4:4** — Abel's firstborn lamb. - **Gen 22:8** — God will provide himself a lamb. - **Exod 12** — Passover lamb. - **Isa 53:7** — Like a…

to.title: Shepherd Throughout Scripture
to.body: # Shepherd Throughout Scripture ## NT Text — John 10; Heb 13:20 Abel, Jacob, Moses, David — all shepherds. YHWH as Shepherd in Psalm 23 and Ezekiel 34. Christ as Good Shepherd who lays down his life. ## OT Antecedents - **Ps 23** — The LORD is my shepherd. - **Ezek 34** — Faithless shepherds vs. true. - **Zech 13:7** — Strike the shepherd. ## Shared structure - Pastoral vocation - Sheep-guidance -…

from.title: Psalm 2 Anointed King
from.body: # Psalm 2 / 'You Are My Son' → Messiah Enthroned Despite Nations' Rage ## OT Story — Psalm 2 Nations rage and kings plot against the LORD and his anointed. The LORD laughs; he installs his King on Zion. 'You are my Son; today I have begotten you. Ask of me, and I will give the nations as your inheritance.' ## NT Fulfillment - Acts 4:25-28: Quoted as fulfilled in the plot against Jesus. - Acts 13:3…

to.title: Psalm 72 Royal Reign
to.body: # Psalm 72 / Solomon's Reign → Messiah's Universal Just Reign ## OT Story — Psalm 72 A psalm of Solomon: he judges with righteousness, defends the poor, kings of Sheba bring gifts, all nations are blessed in him, his name endures forever. ## NT Fulfillment - Matthew 2:11: Magi bring gold, frankincense, myrrh — echo of Sheba. - Revelation 21:24-26: Kings bring glory into the city. - Galatians 3:14:…

from.title: 1 Cor 15 Last Adam
from.body: # 1 Cor 15: Last Adam ## NT Text — 1 Cor 15:45 Christ is the 'last Adam' who became life-giving spirit. The first man was from the earth; the second from heaven. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 2:7** — Adam became a living being. - **Ps 8** — Son of man crowned with glory. - **Isa 53:11** — Bear their iniquities. ## Shared structure - Two Adams - First/last - Earthly/heavenly - Living being/life-giving …

to.title: Adam Christ Two Heads Romans 5
to.body: # Adam and Christ as Two Heads (Rom 5) ## NT Text — Rom 5:12-21 Paul reads humanity as represented by two federal heads: Adam, by whom sin and death entered, and Christ, by whom righteousness and life come. Two acts, two reigns, two destinies. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 2-3** — Adam's covenant headship and fall. - **Ps 51:5** — Inherited sinfulness. - **Hos 6:7** — Like Adam, they transgressed the …

from.title: Raising Of Lazarus
from.body: # Raising of Lazarus ## What happens Four days after Lazarus's death, Jesus arrives at Bethany. He weeps, then calls 'Lazarus, come out' — and the dead man emerges still bound in graveclothes. ## Typological significance The seventh and climactic sign in John. Resurrection in advance of the cross, decisively triggering the conspiracy to kill Jesus. The graveclothes that bind Lazarus contrast with …

to.title: Resurrection Of Christ
to.body: # Resurrection of Christ ## What happens On the third day, Jesus rises bodily from the tomb. The stone is rolled away, the graveclothes left folded, and He appears to His disciples over forty days. ## Typological significance The miracle to which all prior miracles point — the firstfruits of new creation. Every typological pattern (Jonah, Isaac, the parted sea, raised sons, sealed pits) finds here…

from.title: Hosea Gomer Faithless Bride
from.body: # Hosea & Gomer → God's Faithful Love for the Faithless Bride ## OT Story — Hosea 1-3 God commands Hosea: *"Go, take to yourself a wife of whoredom and have children of whoredom, for the land commits great whoredom by forsaking the LORD"* (Hos 1:2). Hosea marries Gomer. She bears children (named *Jezreel*, *Lo-Ruhamah* "Not-Loved," and *Lo-Ammi* "Not-My-People") and then leaves him for other lover…

to.title: Bride Throughout Scripture
to.body: # Bride Throughout Scripture ## NT Text — Eph 5; Rev 19, 21; John 3:29 From Eve to the New Jerusalem, the bride is a pattern of covenant love. Israel as bride, faithless and beloved; the church as bride; the city as bride. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 2:21-24** — Eve formed from Adam's side. - **Hos 1-3** — YHWH's bride. - **Isa 62:5** — Bridegroom rejoices over bride. - **Ezek 16** — Faithless bride…

from.title: Isaiah 53 Suffering Servant
from.body: # Isaiah 53 Suffering Servant → Christ's Passion ## OT Story — Isaiah 52:13-53:12 The fourth and climactic Servant Song. The Servant is *"despised and rejected"* (53:3), *"pierced for our transgressions, crushed for our iniquities"* (53:5). He is silent before his accusers — *"like a lamb that is led to the slaughter, and like a sheep that before its shearers is silent, so he opened not his mouth"…

to.title: Isaiah 50 Third Servant Song
to.body: # Third Servant Song / I Gave My Back → Christ Set His Face ## OT Story — Isaiah 50:4-9 'I gave my back to those who strike, my cheeks to those who pull out the beard… I have set my face like a flint; I know I shall not be put to shame.' ## NT Fulfillment - Luke 9:51: He set his face to go to Jerusalem. - Matthew 26:67: They spat in his face and struck him. - Matthew 27:14: He gave no answer. ## S…

from.title: 1 John 2 Atoning Sacrifice
from.body: # 1 John 2: Atoning Sacrifice ## NT Text — 1 John 2:1-2 If anyone sins we have an advocate with the Father, Jesus Christ the righteous. He is the propitiation for our sins — and for the whole world. ## OT Antecedents - **Lev 16** — Day of Atonement propitiation. - **Isa 53:10** — Soul an offering for sin. - **Ps 32:1-2** — Blessed is he whose sin is covered. ## Shared structure - Advocate - Propit…

to.title: Hebrews 9 Once For All
to.body: # Hebrews 9: Once for All ## NT Text — Heb 9:11-14, 24-28 Christ entered the heavenly sanctuary once for all by his own blood. Annual atonement is superseded by single, eternal redemption. ## OT Antecedents - **Lev 16** — Day of Atonement — annual. - **Exod 26:33** — Veil before the most-holy. - **Zech 9:11** — Blood of the covenant. ## Shared structure - Heavenly sanctuary - Once-for-all entry - …

from.title: Psalm 118 Hosanna
from.body: # Psalm 118 / Hosanna → Triumphal Entry ## OT Story — Psalm 118:25-26 'Save us, we pray, O LORD! Blessed is he who comes in the name of the LORD!' Sung at pilgrimage feasts as Israel ascends to Jerusalem. ## NT Fulfillment - Matthew 21:9: 'Hosanna to the Son of David! Blessed is he who comes…' - John 12:13: Palm branches and the same cry. - Matthew 23:39: 'You will not see me again until you say…'…

to.body: # King on a Donkey → Triumphal Entry ## OT Story — Zechariah 9:9-10 'Rejoice greatly, O daughter of Zion! Behold, your king is coming to you; righteous and having salvation, humble and mounted on a donkey.' ## NT Fulfillment - Matthew 21:4-5: This took place to fulfill what was spoken. - John 12:14-15: Jesus found a young donkey. - Genesis 49:11: Binding his foal to the vine. ## Shared structure -…

from.body: # Gall and Vinegar Offered ## NT Text — Matt 27:34, 48 Jesus is offered gall (refused) and later sour wine (received) — Psalm 69:21 fulfilled. ## OT Antecedents - **Ps 69:21** — Gall and vinegar. - **Prov 31:6** — Strong drink for perishing — refused. ## Shared structure - Bitter drink - Refusal of narcotic - Fulfillment - Mock-mercy ## Theological motif The cup of substitution cannot be drunk dul…

to.title: Psalm 69 Reproaches Fell On Me
to.body: # Psalm 69 / 'Zeal for Your House Has Consumed Me' → Christ's Passion ## OT Story — Psalm 69 The psalmist drowns in deep waters; reproach has broken his heart; they gave him vinegar to drink; zeal for God's house consumes him. ## NT Fulfillment - John 2:17: 'Zeal for your house will consume me' at the temple cleansing. - John 15:25: 'They hated me without cause.' - Romans 15:3: The reproaches of t…

from.title: Samson Birth Annunciation
from.body: # Samson's Birth Foretold → Annunciation Pattern ## OT Story — Judges 13 The angel of the LORD appears to Manoah's barren wife: she will bear a son, a Nazirite from the womb, who will begin to save Israel from the Philistines. ## NT Fulfillment - Luke 1:5-25: Gabriel announces John the Baptist's birth to Zechariah. - Luke 1:26-38: Gabriel announces Jesus' birth to Mary. - Judges 13:18: 'My name is…

to.title: Angel To Manoah Samsons Parents
to.body: # Angel to Manoah and His Wife ## The appearance The angel of YHWH appears to Manoah's barren wife and announces the birth of Samson; on his second visit, when Manoah offers a sacrifice, the angel ascends in the flame of the altar. The angel's name is "Wonderful." ## Mode / context Pre-monarchic Israel under Philistine threat; barren-mother annunciation. ## Significance Annunciation-theophany patt…

from.title: Ezekiel 43 Glory Returns
from.body: # Glory Returns from the East → Christ Returns; God Dwells With Us ## OT Story — Ezekiel 43:1-9 Ezekiel sees the glory of the LORD coming from the east — the same direction it had departed — and filling the new temple. 'I will dwell in their midst forever.' ## NT Fulfillment - Acts 2:1-4: Spirit fills the house with sound from heaven. - Revelation 21:3: God's dwelling is with man. - Matthew 24:27:…

to.body: # Glory of the Latter House → Christ Enters the Second Temple ## OT Story — Haggai 2:6-9 'Yet once more I will shake the heavens and the earth… and the desire of all nations shall come, and I will fill this house with glory… The glory of this latter house shall be greater than the former.' ## NT Fulfillment - Hebrews 12:26-29: Cited as future shaking, leaving only the unshakable. - Luke 21:5-6: Th…

from.title: Cup Of Cup Of Wrath
from.body: # Cup of the Lord's Wrath ## NT Text — Matt 26:39; Mark 14:36 Jesus prays that the 'cup' pass from him. OT 'cup' imagery is consistently the cup of God's wrath against sin. Christ drinks what his people deserved. ## OT Antecedents - **Isa 51:17** — Cup of his wrath, the bowl of staggering. - **Jer 25:15-29** — Cup of wrath given to the nations. - **Ps 75:8** — Cup with foaming wine. ## Shared stru…

to.title: Words From Cross I Thirst
to.body: # Word from Cross: I Thirst ## NT Text — John 19:28 Jesus says 'I thirst' to fulfill Scripture. The Bread-of-Life is consumed by thirst that he might quench ours. ## OT Antecedents - **Ps 69:21** — Vinegar for my thirst. - **Ps 22:15** — Tongue cleaves. - **Isa 53:10** — Soul makes offering for sin. ## Shared structure - Thirst expressed - Sour wine offered - Fulfillment formula - Reversal ## Theo…

from.body: # 1 Cor 15: Firstfruits of Resurrection ## NT Text — 1 Cor 15:20-23 Christ is the 'firstfruits' of those who sleep. The Levitical firstfruits offering guarantees the rest of the harvest. ## OT Antecedents - **Lev 23:9-14** — Sheaf of firstfruits waved before the LORD. - **Deut 26** — Firstfruits ritual. - **Exod 23:19** — Best firstfruits brought to God's house. ## Shared structure - Firstfruits o…

to.body: # 1 Cor 15: Last Adam ## NT Text — 1 Cor 15:45 Christ is the 'last Adam' who became life-giving spirit. The first man was from the earth; the second from heaven. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 2:7** — Adam became a living being. - **Ps 8** — Son of man crowned with glory. - **Isa 53:11** — Bear their iniquities. ## Shared structure - Two Adams - First/last - Earthly/heavenly - Living being/life-giving …

from.title: Joseph In Prison With Two
from.body: # Joseph Between Two Prisoners → Christ Between Two Thieves ## OT Story — Genesis 40 In prison Joseph attends two of Pharaoh's officers — cupbearer and baker. He interprets their dreams: one will be lifted up to life, the other lifted up to death. Both happen on the third day. ## NT Fulfillment - Luke 23:32-43: Christ crucified between two criminals — one to life, one to death. - John 6:35,53-58: …

to.title: Joseph In The Pit
to.body: # Joseph in the Pit → Descent and Deliverance ## OT Story — Genesis 37:18-24 The brothers strip Joseph of his ornate robe and cast him into an empty cistern in the wilderness. From the pit he is drawn up and sold into Egypt. ## NT Fulfillment - Psalm 88:6: 'You have put me in the depths of the pit' — lament that anticipates Christ's descent. - Acts 2:24: Death could not hold him. - Ephesians 4:9-1…

from.title: Garden Throughout Scripture
from.body: # Garden Throughout Scripture ## NT Text — John 18-20; Rev 22 Eden, Song of Songs' enclosed garden, Gethsemane, the resurrection garden, the city-garden of Revelation 22 — gardens trace the arc of communion lost, recovered, consummated. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 2-3** — Eden garden. - **Song 4:12-16** — Sealed garden of love. - **Isa 51:3** — Wilderness made like Eden. - **Ezek 36:35** — Land beco…

to.title: Mountain Throughout Scripture
to.body: # Mountain Throughout Scripture ## NT Text — Heb 12; Matt 5; Rev 21 Mountains structure the canon: Eden's high garden, Moriah, Sinai, Zion, the Mount of Transfiguration, Mount of Olives, the heavenly Mount Zion. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 22** — Moriah. - **Exod 19** — Sinai. - **2 Sam 5:7** — Zion. - **Ezek 28:14** — Holy mountain of God. ## Shared structure - Ascent - Theophany - Covenant - Sacri…

from.title: Revelation 13 Beast From Sea
from.body: # Revelation 13: Beast from the Sea ## NT Text — Rev 13:1-10 A beast rises from the sea — Daniel 7's four beasts combined into one. Political tyranny absorbs the world's worship. ## OT Antecedents - **Dan 7:2-7** — Four beasts from the sea. - **Ps 74:13-14** — Heads of leviathan crushed. - **Isa 27:1** — Leviathan, the twisting serpent. ## Shared structure - Sea origin - Many heads/horns - Blasphe…

to.title: Revelation 17 Babylon The Harlot
to.body: # Revelation 17: Babylon the Harlot ## NT Text — Rev 17 A great harlot sits on many waters, drunken with the blood of the saints. Babylon-Tyre prophecies converge. ## OT Antecedents - **Isa 47** — Lament over Babylon. - **Jer 50-51** — Babylon's fall. - **Ezek 16; 23** — Faithless harlot Jerusalem. ## Shared structure - Harlot imagery - Sitting on waters - Cup of abominations - Adornment in gold -…

from.title: Revelation 4 Throne Vision
from.body: # Revelation 4: Throne Vision ## NT Text — Rev 4 John sees the throne with the rainbow, four living creatures, twenty-four elders — gathering Ezekiel 1, Isaiah 6, and Daniel 7 into one vision. ## OT Antecedents - **Ezek 1** — Four living creatures around the throne. - **Isa 6** — Seraphim and Holy, Holy, Holy. - **Dan 7:9-10** — Ancient of Days enthroned. ## Shared structure - Heavenly throne - Su…

to.body: # Revelation 5: Scroll and Seven Seals ## NT Text — Rev 5:1-7 A scroll sealed with seven seals — none worthy to open. The Lamb takes it. Daniel's sealed words and Ezekiel's scroll converge. ## OT Antecedents - **Ezek 2:9-3:3** — Scroll given to the prophet. - **Dan 12:4** — Seal the book until the time of the end. - **Isa 29:11** — Words of a sealed book. ## Shared structure - Sealed scroll - Sear…

from.title: 1 John 2 Atoning Sacrifice
from.body: # 1 John 2: Atoning Sacrifice ## NT Text — 1 John 2:1-2 If anyone sins we have an advocate with the Father, Jesus Christ the righteous. to.title: 1 Peter 2 He Bore Our Sins
to.body: # 1 Peter 2: He Bore Our Sins ## NT Text — 1 Pet 2:21-25 Christ left an example; he himself bore our sins in his body on the tree, that we might die to sin and live to righteousness. Isaiah 53 is paraphrased throughout. ## OT Antecedents - **Isa 53:4-6** — He bore our griefs; by his wounds we are healed. - **Isa 53:7** — Silent like a lamb. - **Isa 53:11** — Bear their iniquities. ## Shared struct…

from.title: Red Heifer Outside Camp
from.body: # Red Heifer Sacrificed Outside the Camp → Christ Suffered Outside the Gate ## OT Story — Numbers 19 An unblemished red heifer is slaughtered outside the camp; its blood is sprinkled toward the tabernacle, its ashes mixed with water for purification from death-defilement. ## NT Fulfillment - Hebrews 9:13-14: How much more will the blood of Christ purify our conscience. - Hebrews 13:11-13: Let us g…

to.title: Scapegoat Azazel
to.body: # Scapegoat into the Wilderness → Sin Borne Away ## OT Story — Leviticus 16:20-22 The high priest lays both hands on the live goat, confesses Israel's sins over it, and a fit man leads it to a solitary place. The goat bears all iniquities away. ## NT Fulfillment - John 1:29: The Lamb of God who takes away the sin of the world. - Isaiah 53:6: The LORD has laid on him the iniquity of us all. - Hebre…

from.title: Plague Of Firstborn Spared By Blood
from.body: # Plague of the Firstborn / Blood on the Door → Christ's Blood Spares ## OT Story — Exodus 12:21-30 Israel slaughters the Passover lamb, strikes its blood on the doorposts, and stays inside. The destroyer passes over houses marked by blood. Egypt's firstborn die. ## NT Fulfillment - 1 Corinthians 5:7: Christ our Passover lamb has been sacrificed. - 1 Peter 1:18-19: Redeemed by the precious blood o…

to.title: Ten Plagues Of Egypt
to.body: # Ten Plagues of Egypt ## What happens YHWH systematically dismantles Egypt's pantheon through ten escalating signs, culminating in the death of the firstborn averted only by blood on the doorposts. ## Typological significance Cosmic de-creation reversed against the oppressor and the inaugural Passover sacrifice — prototype of redemption by substitutionary blood. The pattern recurs in Revelation's…

to.body: # Day of the LORD / Feet on the Mount of Olives → Christ Returns ## OT Story — Zechariah 14:1-9 'On that day his feet shall stand on the Mount of Olives, which is east of Jerusalem, and the mount shall be split in two… The LORD will be king over all the earth. from.title: Melchizedek
from.body: # Melchizedek → Christ as Eternal King-Priest A typology developed at length in **Hebrews 7**, anchored on a single verse from Psalm 110 and a brief Genesis episode. The argument is structural: Melchizedek's *absence* of genealogy, his combination of *kingly and priestly* offices, and his appearance *before the Levitical priesthood existed* together prefigure Christ. ## OT Story — Genesis 14:17-20…

to.title: Esther Intercessor
to.body: # Esther → Christ as Intercessor ## OT Story — Esther 4-8 Haman, the king's vizier, has secured a decree to annihilate the Jews throughout the Persian empire (Esth 3:13). Mordecai sends word to Esther — now queen — asking her to plead for her people. The problem: anyone who approaches the king unsummoned faces death *"unless the king holds out the golden scepter"* (Esth 4:11). Mordecai's challenge…

from.title: 1 Peter 3 Baptism Like Flood
from.body: # 1 Peter 3: Baptism Like the Flood ## NT Text — 1 Pet 3:18-22 Eight souls were saved through water in Noah's ark; baptism corresponds to this. The flood typifies the cleansing-and-deliverance of Christian baptism. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 7-8** — Noah's flood and ark. - **Gen 6:5-8** — Wickedness met by judgment. - **Ezek 14:14** — Noah, Daniel, and Job — paradigmatic righteous. ## Shared struct…

to.title: 1 Peter 3 Christ Proclaimed To Spirits

from.title: Revelation 14 Lamb On Zion
from.body: # Revelation 14: Lamb on Mount Zion ## NT Text — Rev 14:1-5 The Lamb stands on Mount Zion with 144,000 bearing his Father's name on their foreheads. to.title: Revelation 4 Throne Vision
to.body: # Revelation 4: Throne Vision ## NT Text — Rev 4 John sees the throne with the rainbow, four living creatures, twenty-four elders — gathering Ezekiel 1, Isaiah 6, and Daniel 7 into one vision. ## OT Antecedents - **Ezek 1** — Four living creatures around the throne. - **Isa 6** — Seraphim and Holy, Holy, Holy. - **Dan 7:9-10** — Ancient of Days enthroned. ## Shared structure - Heavenly throne - Su…

to.title: Words From Cross Into Your Hands
to.body: # Word from Cross: Into Your Hands I Commit ## NT Text — Luke 23:46 Jesus dies praying Psalm 31:5 — a Davidic prayer of trust. Stephen later echoes it. Death is recast as commendation. ## OT Antecedents - **Ps 31:5** — Into your hand I commit my spirit. - **Eccl 12:7** — Spirit returns to God. - **Acts 7:59** — Stephen echoes this prayer. ## Shared structure - Spirit-commendation - Trust in Father…

to.title: Titus 2 Blessed Hope
to.body: # Titus 2: Blessed Hope ## NT Text — Titus 2:11-14 Grace appears teaching us to live godly, awaiting the blessed hope — the appearing of the glory of our great God and Savior Jesus Christ. ## OT Antecedents - **Ps 67** — God's saving way among all nations. - **Isa 40:5** — Glory of the LORD revealed. - **Mal 3:1** — Lord whom you seek will come. ## Shared structure - Grace appeared (incarnation) -…

from.title: Pierced Side Blood And Water
from.body: # Pierced Side: Blood and Water ## NT Text — John 19:34-37 A soldier pierces Jesus' side; blood and water flow. John cites Zechariah 12:10. ## OT Antecedents - **Zech 12:10** — They will look on him whom they pierced. - **Exod 17:6** — Water from struck rock. - **Ezek 47:1** — Water from temple. ## Shared structure - Pierced side - Blood and water - Look-and-mourn - Temple-water echo ## Theologica…

to.title: Words From Cross I Thirst
to.body: # Word from Cross: I Thirst ## NT Text — John 19:28 Jesus says 'I thirst' to fulfill Scripture. The Bread-of-Life is consumed by thirst that he might quench ours. ## OT Antecedents - **Ps 69:21** — Vinegar for my thirst. - **Ps 22:15** — Tongue cleaves. - **Isa 53:10** — Soul makes offering for sin. ## Shared structure - Thirst expressed - Sour wine offered - Fulfillment formula - Reversal ## Theo…

from.title: Bones Not Broken
from.body: # Bones Not Broken ## NT Text — John 19:33-36 Jesus' legs are not broken; John cites the Passover-lamb statute. The true Passover Lamb dies in conformity to the Exodus rite. ## OT Antecedents - **Exod 12:46** — Not a bone shall be broken. - **Num 9:12** — Same rule for Passover. - **Ps 34:20** — He keeps all his bones. ## Shared structure - Unbroken bones - Passover statute - Preservation amid dea…

to.title: Last Supper As Passover
to.body: # Last Supper as Passover ## NT Text — Matt 26:17-30; Mark 14:12-26; Luke 22:7-23 The Last Supper is celebrated as a Passover meal, with Jesus reinterpreting bread and cup as his body and blood. The Exodus typology comes to its fulfillment: the true Passover Lamb is at table. ## OT Antecedents - **Exod 12** — The Passover meal in Egypt. - **Exod 24:8** — Blood of the covenant sprinkled on the peop…

from.title: 1 Peter 2 He Bore Our Sins
from.body: # 1 Peter 2: He Bore Our Sins ## NT Text — 1 Pet 2:21-25 Christ left an example; he himself bore our sins in his body on the tree, that we might die to sin and live to righteousness. Isaiah 53 is paraphrased throughout. ## OT Antecedents - **Isa 53:4-6** — He bore our griefs; by his wounds we are healed. - **Isa 53:7** — Silent like a lamb. - **Isa 53:11** — Bear their iniquities. ## Shared struct…

to.title: Galatians 3 Curse Of Law
to.body: # Galatians 3: Curse of the Law ## NT Text — Gal 3:10-14 Christ became a curse for us, hanging on a tree — fulfilling Deut 21:23 and Deut 27:26. The curse of the law is borne so that Abrahamic blessing flows to the nations. ## OT Antecedents - **Deut 21:23** — Cursed is everyone hanged on a tree. - **Deut 27:26** — Cursed is anyone who does not uphold this law. - **Gen 12:3** — Abrahamic blessing …

from.title: Great Commission
from.body: # Great Commission ## NT Text — Matt 28:16-20 Jesus on a Galilean mountain commissions disciples to make disciples of all nations. Daniel-7 authority enacted in mission. ## OT Antecedents - **Dan 7:13-14** — All peoples should serve him. - **Gen 12:3** — In you all nations blessed. - **Isa 49:6** — Light to nations. ## Shared structure - Mountain - Universal authority - Universal mission - Trinita…

to.title: Mountain Throughout Scripture
to.body: # Mountain Throughout Scripture ## NT Text — Heb 12; Matt 5; Rev 21 Mountains structure the canon: Eden's high garden, Moriah, Sinai, Zion, the Mount of Transfiguration, Mount of Olives, the heavenly Mount Zion. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 22** — Moriah. - **Exod 19** — Sinai. - **2 Sam 5:7** — Zion. - **Ezek 28:14** — Holy mountain of God. ## Shared structure - Ascent - Theophany - Covenant - Sacri…

from.title: Garden Of Gethsemane As Eden Reversed
from.body: # Garden of Gethsemane as Eden Reversed ## NT Text — Matt 26:36-46; John 18:1 Jesus enters a garden to face the will of his Father. Where Adam in the garden chose his own will and brought death, the last Adam chooses the Father's will and accepts death to bring life. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 2-3** — Eden — the garden of disobedience. - **Gen 3:17-19** — Toil and sweat as curse. - **2 Sam 15:23** …

to.title: Mary Magdalene First Witness
to.body: # Mary Magdalene First Witness ## NT Text — John 20:11-18 The first resurrection witness is a woman in a garden who mistakes Jesus for the gardener. Eden is reopened. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 3** — Eve in the garden. - **Song 3:1-4** — I sought him whom my soul loves. - **Gen 2:15** — Adam to work and keep the garden. ## Shared structure - Garden setting - Woman seeker - Risen Christ as gardener …

from.title: adon
from.body: # adon (אָדוֹן) — lord / master ## Semantic field Lord, master, owner — used of human authority and, in the form Adonai, of God. Used to verbally substitute for YHWH when the divine name is encountered. ## Theological weight Psalm 110:1 ("YHWH said to my adon") becomes the most-cited OT verse in the NT, applied to Christ. The exalted Christ is the adon at YHWH's right hand. ## Septuagint mapping T…

to.title: kyrios
to.body: # kyrios (κύριος) — lord ## Semantic field Lord, master, owner. Used for ordinary masters but also as the LXX rendering of YHWH and Adonai. ## Theological weight "Iesous Kyrios" — "Jesus is Lord" — is the earliest Christian confession (Rom 10:9). Because kyrios translates YHWH in LXX, the confession is implicit assertion of Jesus' divine identity. ## Septuagint mapping Renders both YHWH and adon. …

from.title: Water Throughout Scripture
from.body: # Water Throughout Scripture ## NT Text — John 4; John 7; Rev 22 Creation's waters, flood, Red Sea, wilderness rocks, Jordan, the Samaritan well, baptism, the river of life — water marks judgment and life. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 1:2** — Spirit hovering over waters. - **Exod 14** — Red Sea passage. - **Ezek 47** — River from the temple. ## Shared structure - Chaos-water - Judgment-water - Delive…

to.title: Wedding Throughout Scripture
to.body: # Wedding Throughout Scripture ## NT Text — John 2; Rev 19 From Adam's awakening to Eve to Cana's water-turned-wine to the marriage supper of the Lamb, weddings mark new-creation thresholds. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 2:22-24** — First wedding. - **Song 3:11** — Day of Solomon's wedding. - **Isa 62:5** — Like a bridegroom rejoices. ## Shared structure - Anticipation - Feast - Joy - Covenant initiat…

from.title: nephesh
from.body: # nephesh (נֶפֶשׁ) — soul / living being ## Semantic field The whole living self — not an immaterial soul opposed to body, but the integrated living creature. Animals are also nephesh chayyah. Sometimes refers concretely to "throat" or "appetite." ## Theological weight "The life (nephesh) of the flesh is in the blood" (Lev 17:11) — undergirds atonement theology. Genesis 2:7 names humanity as nephe…

to.title: psyche
to.body: # psyche (ψυχή) — soul / life ## Semantic field Soul, life, the self. In Greek philosophy often dualistically opposed to body; in NT (especially when translating nephesh) more integrative, naming the whole living person. ## Theological weight "What does it profit to gain the world and forfeit his psyche?" (Mark 8:36). The psyche can be lost or saved; Jesus gives His psyche as ransom (Mark 10:45). …

from.title: Boaz Ruth
from.body: # Boaz and Ruth → Christ the Kinsman-Redeemer The book of Ruth is structured as an extended illustration of the *goel* (kinsman-redeemer) law in Leviticus 25. It is also the only OT book named after a Gentile, foreshadowing the inclusion of the nations in Christ. ## OT Story — Ruth 2-4 Naomi loses her husband and both sons in Moab. She returns to Bethlehem as a widow, with her **Moabite (Gentile)*…

to.title: Judah Tamar Levirate
to.body: # Judah and Tamar / Levirate → Messianic Line Preserved ## OT Story — Genesis 38 Tamar, widowed and denied levirate marriage, disguises herself and conceives twins by Judah. Judah confesses, 'She is more righteous than I.' Perez is born — ancestor of David and Jesus. ## NT Fulfillment - Matthew 1:3: Tamar named in Jesus' genealogy. - Ruth 4:18-22: Perez's line leads to David. - Romans 5:20: Where …

from.title: Day Of Atonement High Priest
from.body: # High Priest on the Day of Atonement → Christ's Greater Entry ## OT Story — Leviticus 16 Once a year the high priest, after washing and offering for his own sin, enters the most holy place with blood, sprinkles the mercy seat, and emerges to bless the people. ## NT Fulfillment - Hebrews 9:6-12: Christ entered once for all, with his own blood, into the greater tent not made with hands. - Hebrews 4…

to.title: Scapegoat Azazel
to.body: # Scapegoat into the Wilderness → Sin Borne Away ## OT Story — Leviticus 16:20-22 The high priest lays both hands on the live goat, confesses Israel's sins over it, and a fit man leads it to a solitary place. The goat bears all iniquities away. ## NT Fulfillment - John 1:29: The Lamb of God who takes away the sin of the world. - Isaiah 53:6: The LORD has laid on him the iniquity of us all. - Hebre…

from.title: Asenath Gentile Bride
from.body: # Asenath / Gentile Bride → Christ's Bride from the Nations ## OT Story — Genesis 41:45 While rejected by his brothers and exalted in Egypt, Joseph is given an Egyptian wife, Asenath. From her come Manasseh and Ephraim, brought into the covenant family. ## NT Fulfillment - Ephesians 3:6: Gentiles are fellow heirs, members of the same body. - Ephesians 5:31-32: Marriage as Christ-and-church mystery…

to.title: Bride Throughout Scripture
to.body: # Bride Throughout Scripture ## NT Text — Eph 5; Rev 19, 21; John 3:29 From Eve to the New Jerusalem, the bride is a pattern of covenant love. Israel as bride, faithless and beloved; the church as bride; the city as bride. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 2:21-24** — Eve formed from Adam's side. - **Hos 1-3** — YHWH's bride. - **Isa 62:5** — Bridegroom rejoices over bride. - **Ezek 16** — Faithless bride…

from.title: Garden Eden Cherubim
from.body: # Eden's Cherubim → Christ as the Way Back ## OT Story — Genesis 3:22-24 After the fall, God says: *"Behold, the man has become like one of us in knowing good and evil. Now, lest he reach out his hand and take also of the tree of life and eat, and live forever..."* (Gen 3:22). He drives the man out, and *"at the east of the garden of Eden he placed the **cherubim** and a **flaming sword** that tur…

to.title: Jacobs Ladder
to.body: # Jacob's Ladder → Jesus as the Ladder (His Own Citation) ## OT Story — Genesis 28:10-22 Jacob is fleeing his brother Esau, who wants to kill him for the stolen blessing. Alone, in the open, he stops *"because the sun had set"*. He takes a stone for a pillow and lies down. He dreams: *"behold, a **ladder** [*sullam* — more accurately a stairway/ramp] set up on the earth, and the **top of it reache…

from.title: Pierced One Zechariah
from.body: # Pierced One (Zechariah 12:10) ## The oracle YHWH pours out a spirit of grace and supplication on Jerusalem so that they look on "me whom they have pierced" and mourn for him as for an only son. ## Date / context Post-exilic oracle of Zechariah, late 6th c. BC, looking to a future day of repentance. ## Fulfillment John applies it to the spear-thrust at the cross (John 19:34-37); Revelation 1:7 us…

to.title: Smitten By God
to.body: # Smitten by God ## The oracle "We esteemed him stricken, smitten by God, and afflicted. But he was pierced for our transgressions; upon him was the chastisement that brought us peace." ## Date / context Isaiah 53, exilic horizon; the chastened reflection of those who had misjudged the Servant. ## Fulfillment The cry of dereliction (Matthew 27:46) and the Petrine commentary (1 Peter 2:24) read the…

from.title: basar
from.body: # basar (בָּשָׂר) — flesh ## Semantic field Physical flesh — human or animal — and by extension kinship ("my bone and my flesh") and creaturely frailty ("all flesh is grass," Isa 40:6). ## Theological weight Basar names creaturely embodiment in its fragility. The incarnation (NT sarx, John 1:14) translates this: the Word becomes basar, the divine entering creaturely weakness. ## Septuagint mapping…

to.title: sarx
to.body: # sarx (σάρξ) — flesh ## Semantic field Flesh — neutral creaturely embodiment ("the Word became sarx"), but in Paul also a moral category: the fallen orientation of the self in opposition to the Spirit. ## Theological weight John 1:14: the Logos becomes sarx — God in creaturely embodiment. Paul's "according to the sarx vs according to the pneuma" names the eschatological conflict in the believer. …

from.title: David And Mephibosheth Kindness
from.body: # David and Mephibosheth → Hesed Toward the Disabled Enemy's Son ## OT Story — 2 Samuel 9 David seeks anyone of Saul's house to show kindness for Jonathan's sake. Mephibosheth — lame, in exile — is summoned, set at the king's table 'as one of the king's sons.' ## NT Fulfillment - Ephesians 2:4-7: God raised us up with Christ and seated us in the heavenly places. - Luke 14:15-24: Bring in the poor,…

to.title: David Spares Saul
to.body: # David Spares Saul → Christ Forbearing With Persecutors ## OT Story — 1 Samuel 24, 26 Twice David could kill Saul who is hunting him. He refuses: 'I will not put forth my hand against the LORD's anointed.' He waits on God to vindicate. ## NT Fulfillment - Romans 12:17-21: Do not avenge yourselves; leave it to the wrath of God. - 1 Peter 2:23: When reviled, he did not revile; he entrusted himself …

from.title: Malachi Messenger Of Covenant
from.body: # Malachi 3 / Messenger Prepares the Way → John the Baptist ## OT Story — Malachi 3:1 'Behold, I send my messenger, and he will prepare the way before me. And the Lord whom you seek will suddenly come to his temple — the messenger of the covenant, in whom you delight.' ## NT Fulfillment - Mark 1:2: 'Behold, I send my messenger before your face.' - Matthew 11:10: Jesus identifies John as this messe…

to.title: Malachi Refiners Fire
to.body: # Malachi 3 / Refiner's Fire → Trials Purifying the Saints ## OT Story — Malachi 3:2-4 'Who can endure the day of his coming? For he is like a refiner's fire and like fullers' soap… He will purify the sons of Levi and refine them like gold and silver.' ## NT Fulfillment - 1 Peter 1:6-7: Tested by fire — your faith more precious than gold. - 1 Corinthians 3:12-15: Each one's work tested by fire. - …

from.title: Hebrews 5 Order Of Melchizedek
from.body: # Hebrews 5: Order of Melchizedek ## NT Text — Heb 5:5-10 Christ does not glorify himself but is appointed by God as high priest after the order of Melchizedek. Psalm 110:4 is the controlling text. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 14:18-20** — Melchizedek priest-king greets Abraham. - **Ps 110:4** — Priest forever after the order of Melchizedek. - **Isa 53:12** — Servant's exaltation through suffering. #…

to.title: Hebrews 9 Mediator Of New Covenant
to.body: # Hebrews 9: Mediator of the New Covenant ## NT Text — Heb 9:15-22 Christ is mediator of a new covenant. Where a covenant exists, the death of the covenant-maker must be established. Sinai's blood-sprinkling foreshadowed Calvary's. ## OT Antecedents - **Exod 24:6-8** — Moses sprinkles blood on the people. - **Jer 31:33** — New covenant. - **Gen 15:9-21** — Covenant-cutting with Abraham. ## Shared …

from.title: Psalm 23 Shepherd
from.body: # Psalm 23 / The LORD My Shepherd → Christ the Good Shepherd ## OT Story — Psalm 23 'The LORD is my shepherd, I shall not want… he restores my soul… valley of the shadow of death… your rod and staff comfort me… you prepare a table before me.' ## NT Fulfillment - John 10:11: I am the good shepherd; the good shepherd lays down his life. - Revelation 7:17: The Lamb at the center of the throne will sh…

to.title: Psalm 8 Son Of Man
to.body: # Psalm 8 / Son of Man Crowned → Christ's Dominion Restored ## OT Story — Psalm 8 'What is man that you are mindful of him, the son of man that you care for him?' Made a little lower than the heavenly beings, crowned with glory, all things under his feet. ## NT Fulfillment - Hebrews 2:5-9: Applied to Jesus — we see Jesus crowned with glory and honor. - 1 Corinthians 15:27: All things put in subjec…

from.title: Romans 11 Olive Tree Grafted
from.body: # Romans 11: Olive Tree Grafted ## NT Text — Rom 11:17-24 Paul portrays Israel as an olive tree whose natural branches were broken off and wild Gentile branches grafted in. The hope is restoration of all Israel. ## OT Antecedents - **Jer 11:16** — Green olive tree, fair with goodly fruit. - **Isa 17:6** — Gleanings of olive harvest. - **Hos 14:6** — Beauty like the olive. ## Shared structure - Oli…

to.title: Tree Throughout Scripture
to.body: # Tree Throughout Scripture ## NT Text — Acts 5:30; Gal 3:13; Rev 22 Eden's two trees, Abraham's oaks, Israel's vine and olive, the tree of curse on which Christ hung, the tree of life in Revelation. Trees thread sin and salvation. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 2:9** — Tree of life and tree of knowledge. - **Gen 21:33** — Abraham's tamarisk. - **Deut 21:23** — Cursed is anyone hung on a tree. - **Ezek…

from.title: Zechariah 14 Day Of The LORD
from.body: # Day of the LORD / Feet on the Mount of Olives → Christ Returns ## OT Story — Zechariah 14:1-9 'On that day his feet shall stand on the Mount of Olives, which is east of Jerusalem, and the mount shall be split in two… The LORD will be king over all the earth. On that day the LORD will be one and his name one.' ## NT Fulfillment - Acts 1:11-12: Angels say Jesus will return as he went; the place wa…

to.title: Zechariah 9 King On Donkey
to.body: # King on a Donkey → Triumphal Entry ## OT Story — Zechariah 9:9-10 'Rejoice greatly, O daughter of Zion! Behold, your king is coming to you; righteous and having salvation, humble and mounted on a donkey.' ## NT Fulfillment - Matthew 21:4-5: This took place to fulfill what was spoken. - John 12:14-15: Jesus found a young donkey. - Genesis 49:11: Binding his foal to the vine. ## Shared structure -…

from.title: 1 Peter 2 He Bore Our Sins
from.body: # 1 Peter 2: He Bore Our Sins ## NT Text — 1 Pet 2:21-25 Christ left an example; he himself bore our sins in his body on the tree, that we might die to sin and live to righteousness. Isaiah 53 is paraphrased throughout. ## OT Antecedents - **Isa 53:4-6** — He bore our griefs; by his wounds we are healed. - **Isa 53:7** — Silent like a lamb. - **Isa 53:11** — Bear their iniquities. ## Shared struct…

to.title: Hebrews 13 Outside The Camp
to.body: # Hebrews 13: Outside the Camp ## NT Text — Heb 13:11-14 Jesus suffered outside the gate to sanctify the people through his own blood. Let us go to him outside the camp, bearing his reproach. ## OT Antecedents - **Lev 16:27** — Sin-offering carcasses burned outside the camp. - **Num 19:3** — Red heifer slain outside the camp. - **Exod 33:7** — Tent of meeting pitched outside the camp. ## Shared st…

from.title: euangelion
from.body: # euangelion (εὐαγγέλιον) — good news ## Semantic field Good announcement, glad message. In Greco-Roman world used for imperial proclamations (birth, victory of Caesar); NT polemically claims it for Jesus' lordship. ## Theological weight "The beginning of the euangelion of Jesus Christ, Son of God" (Mark 1:1). The euangelion is the announcement that the crucified-and-risen Jesus is Lord — Israel's…

to.title: evangelizo
to.body: # evangelizo (εὐαγγελίζω) — proclaim good news ## Semantic field To announce/proclaim good news. Verb form of euangelion. Active proclamation, not silent good-feeling. ## Theological weight Jesus' Nazareth manifesto: "He has anointed me to evangelizo the poor" (Luke 4:18). The early church evangelizo as their default action. Faith comes by hearing this announced word. ## Septuagint mapping Renders…

from.title: Ascension
from.body: # Ascension ## What happens Forty days after the resurrection, Jesus leads the disciples to Bethany, blesses them, and is taken up in a cloud; angels promise He will return in the same way. ## Typological significance Enthronement of the Son of Man as Daniel 7 foresaw — coming with clouds to the Ancient of Days. Elijah's whirlwind translation is fulfilled in the bodily enthronement of Christ. ## C…

to.title: Resurrection Of Christ
to.body: # Resurrection of Christ ## What happens On the third day, Jesus rises bodily from the tomb. The stone is rolled away, the graveclothes left folded, and He appears to His disciples over forty days. ## Typological significance The miracle to which all prior miracles point — the firstfruits of new creation. Every typological pattern (Jonah, Isaac, the parted sea, raised sons, sealed pits) finds here…

from.title: 2 Cor 3 Veil Lifted In Christ
from.body: # 2 Cor 3: Veil Lifted in Christ ## NT Text — 2 Cor 3:7-18 Moses' veil over his face symbolizes the old covenant's partial reading. In Christ the veil is taken away and believers behold his glory unveiled. ## OT Antecedents - **Exod 34:29-35** — Moses' face shines; he veils it. - **Jer 31:33** — Law written on the heart. - **Ezek 36:26** — New heart and Spirit. ## Shared structure - Veil of glory …

to.title: Colossians 2 Buried And Raised With Christ
to.body: # Colossians 2: Buried and Raised with Christ ## NT Text — Col 2:11-13 Believers are circumcised with a circumcision made without hands, buried with Christ in baptism, raised with him through faith. Old self stripped, new self received. ## OT Antecedents - **Deut 30:6** — Circumcise your heart. - **Jer 4:4** — Circumcise yourselves to the LORD. - **Ezek 36:26** — New heart and new spirit. ## Share…

from.title: ish
from.body: # ish (אִישׁ) — man / husband ## Semantic field An individual man, husband, or specific person (in contrast to adam = humanity). The wordplay with ishah (woman) in Gen 2:23 marks marital correspondence. ## Theological weight Genesis 2 establishes ish/ishah complementarity at creation. "A certain man" repeatedly initiates patriarchal-narrative episodes; ish is also a way of referring to mysterious …

to.title: ishah
to.body: # ishah (אִשָּׁה) — woman / wife ## Semantic field Woman, wife — counterpart to ish. The wordplay in Genesis 2:23 marks covenantal correspondence at creation. ## Theological weight The protoevangelium promises that the seed of the ishah will crush the serpent (Gen 3:15). The woman is mother of the living, suffering bearer of promise, and (in NT extension) image of the church as bride. ## Septuagin…

from.title: Revelation 1 Son Of Man Vision
from.body: # Revelation 1: Son of Man Vision ## NT Text — Rev 1:12-18 John sees one 'like a son of man' among golden lampstands — eyes like fire, feet like burnished bronze, voice like many waters. Daniel 7 and 10 are gathered. ## OT Antecedents - **Dan 7:13** — One like a son of man. - **Dan 10:5-6** — Man clothed in linen, eyes like fire. - **Ezek 1:7** — Feet like burnished bronze. ## Shared structure - A…

to.title: Revelation 21 New Jerusalem Descending
to.body: # Revelation 21: New Jerusalem Descending ## NT Text — Rev 21:1-22:5 John sees the new heaven and new earth and the New Jerusalem descending from heaven as a bride adorned. God dwells with men. ## OT Antecedents - **Isa 65:17-25** — New heavens and new earth. - **Ezek 40-48** — Vision of restored temple-city. - **Zech 8:3** — I will dwell in Zion. ## Shared structure - New cosmos - City descending…

from.title: Hosea Three Days Raised Up
from.body: # Hosea 6 / On the Third Day He Will Raise Us → Resurrection on the Third Day ## OT Story — Hosea 6:1-3 'Come, let us return to the LORD; for he has torn us, and he will heal us. After two days he will revive us; on the third day he will raise us up, that we may live before him.' ## NT Fulfillment - 1 Corinthians 15:4: Raised on the third day in accordance with the Scriptures. - Luke 24:46: It is …

to.title: Jonah Three Days In Fish
to.body: # Jonah Three Days in the Fish → Sign of Jonah, Christ's Burial ## OT Story — Jonah 1-2 Jonah, fleeing, is swallowed by a great fish for three days and three nights. From the belly of Sheol he prays; he is vomited out alive to preach to Nineveh. ## NT Fulfillment - Matthew 12:38-41: As Jonah was three days, so will the Son of Man be. - Luke 11:29-30: The sign of Jonah. - Jonah 2:6: I went down to …

from.title: Isaiah 49 Second Servant Song
from.body: # Second Servant Song / Light to Nations → Christ to the Gentiles ## OT Story — Isaiah 49:1-13 'The LORD called me from the womb… It is too light a thing that you should be my servant to raise up the tribes of Jacob; I will make you a light for the nations.' ## NT Fulfillment - Acts 13:47: Paul cites it as the apostolic commission. - Luke 2:30-32: Simeon — 'a light for revelation to the Gentiles.'…

to.title: Isaiah 9 Child Is Born
to.body: # Isaiah 9 / 'For Unto Us a Child Is Born' → Eternal King ## OT Story — Isaiah 9:6-7 'A child is born, a son is given; the government shall be upon his shoulder. His name shall be called Wonderful Counselor, Mighty God, Everlasting Father, Prince of Peace.' ## NT Fulfillment - Luke 1:32-33: He will be great and called Son of the Most High; his kingdom no end. - Luke 2:11: Born this day a Savior, C…

from.title: Jonah Repentant Nineveh
from.body: # Nineveh Repents → Gentile Repentance Shames Israel ## OT Story — Jonah 3 Jonah preaches: 'Forty days and Nineveh shall be overthrown.' The whole city, from king to cattle, fasts and repents. God relents. ## NT Fulfillment - Matthew 12:41: Men of Nineveh will rise at the judgment and condemn this generation. - Acts 17:30-31: God now commands all people everywhere to repent. - Romans 10:11-13: No …

to.title: Manasseh Repentance
to.body: # Manasseh's Repentance → Mercy for the Worst ## OT Story — 2 Chronicles 33:10-17 Manasseh, Judah's most wicked king, is taken captive to Babylon in fetters. There he humbles himself greatly; God hears and restores him. ## NT Fulfillment - Luke 23:42-43: The thief on the cross — today you will be with me in paradise. - 1 Timothy 1:15-16: Christ saves the worst, Paul as exemplar. - Isaiah 1:18: Tho…

from.title: Light Throughout Scripture
from.body: # Light Throughout Scripture ## NT Text — John 1; Rev 21 Genesis's first creative word is 'let there be light'; the prophets promise light to the nations; Christ is the Light of the World; the New Jerusalem needs no lamp. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 1:3** — Let there be light. - **Isa 9:2** — People in darkness have seen great light. - **Isa 60:1-3** — Arise, shine. ## Shared structure - Creation li…

to.title: Revelation 1 Son Of Man Vision
to.body: # Revelation 1: Son of Man Vision ## NT Text — Rev 1:12-18 John sees one 'like a son of man' among golden lampstands — eyes like fire, feet like burnished bronze, voice like many waters. Daniel 7 and 10 are gathered. ## OT Antecedents - **Dan 7:13** — One like a son of man. - **Dan 10:5-6** — Man clothed in linen, eyes like fire. - **Ezek 1:7** — Feet like burnished bronze. ## Shared structure - A…

from.title: Gall And Vinegar Offered
from.body: # Gall and Vinegar Offered ## NT Text — Matt 27:34, 48 Jesus is offered gall (refused) and later sour wine (received) — Psalm 69:21 fulfilled. ## OT Antecedents - **Ps 69:21** — Gall and vinegar. - **Prov 31:6** — Strong drink for perishing — refused. ## Shared structure - Bitter drink - Refusal of narcotic - Fulfillment - Mock-mercy ## Theological motif The cup of substitution cannot be drunk dul…

to.title: Wine Throughout Scripture
to.body: # Wine Throughout Scripture ## NT Text — John 2; Matt 26 Noah's vineyard, Melchizedek's wine, Passover cups, Isaiah's wine-feast, Cana's water-into-wine, the cup of the new covenant, the cup of wrath — wine threads the canon. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 9:20** — Noah's vineyard. - **Gen 14:18** — Melchizedek's bread and wine. - **Isa 25:6** — Aged wine on the mountain. - **Jer 25:15** — Cup of wrath…

from.title: Absalom Hung On Tree
from.body: # Absalom Hangs in a Tree → Cursed One on the Tree ## OT Story — 2 Samuel 18:9-15 Absalom's hair catches in the branches of a great oak; he hangs between heaven and earth while his mule rides on. Joab pierces him with three darts. ## NT Fulfillment - Galatians 3:13: Cursed is everyone who hangs on a tree. - Deuteronomy 21:22-23: A hanged man is cursed by God. - John 19:34: Pierced in the side. ## …

to.title: Crown Of Thorns Curse Reversed
to.body: # Crown of Thorns: Curse Reversed ## NT Text — Matt 27:29; John 19:2 Soldiers crown Jesus with thorns. The symbol of Adam's curse becomes the messianic crown — the king bears the curse on his head. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 3:17-18** — Thorns and thistles as curse. - **Num 33:55** — Thorns in your sides. - **Ezek 28:24** — No more brier or thorn for Israel. ## Shared structure - Curse-symbol - Roy…

from.title: Hebrews 12 Looking To Jesus
from.body: # Hebrews 12: Looking to Jesus ## NT Text — Heb 12:1-3 Lay aside every weight; run with endurance the race, looking to Jesus, the founder and perfecter of faith. ## OT Antecedents - **Ps 16:8** — I have set the LORD before me. - **Isa 50:7** — I have set my face like a flint. - **Prov 4:25** — Let your eyes look directly forward. ## Shared structure - Weight removed - Sin discarded - Endurance rac…

to.title: Hebrews 12 Mount Zion Not Sinai
to.body: # Hebrews 12: Mount Zion, Not Sinai ## NT Text — Heb 12:18-24 You have not come to Sinai with its terror but to Mount Zion, to the heavenly Jerusalem, to angels, to the assembly of the firstborn, to Jesus the mediator. ## OT Antecedents - **Exod 19** — Sinai theophany of terror. - **Ps 48** — Zion's beauty. - **Isa 60** — Zion glorified. ## Shared structure - Sinai/terror contrast - Zion/grace des…

from.title: Judah Tamar Levirate
from.body: # Judah and Tamar / Levirate → Messianic Line Preserved ## OT Story — Genesis 38 Tamar, widowed and denied levirate marriage, disguises herself and conceives twins by Judah. Judah confesses, 'She is more righteous than I.' Perez is born — ancestor of David and Jesus. ## NT Fulfillment - Matthew 1:3: Tamar named in Jesus' genealogy. - Ruth 4:18-22: Perez's line leads to David. - Romans 5:20: Where …

to.title: Reuben Defiled Bed
to.body: # Reuben Loses the Birthright → Firstborn Privilege Forfeited ## OT Story — Genesis 35:22; 49:3-4 Reuben, the natural firstborn, lies with his father's concubine. In Jacob's deathbed blessing he is told, 'Unstable as water, you shall not have preeminence.' Birthright passes to Joseph, kingship to Judah. ## NT Fulfillment - Hebrews 12:16-17: Like Esau, those who despise the holy lose the inheritanc…

from.title: Gibeonite Deception
from.body: # Gibeonite Deception → Discernment and Covenant Faithfulness ## OT Story — Joshua 9 The Gibeonites dress in worn clothing and pretend to be from afar. Israel makes a covenant without asking the LORD. When the deceit is discovered, the covenant stands; they become servants in God's house. ## NT Fulfillment - Matthew 10:16: Wise as serpents, innocent as doves. - 2 Corinthians 11:14: Satan disguises…

to.title: Spies Twelve
to.body: # Twelve Spies / Two Faithful → Witnesses of the Promise ## OT Story — Numbers 13 Twelve spies survey Canaan. Ten report giants; two — Caleb and Joshua — say, 'We can surely go up.' Israel believes the ten and forfeits the land for forty years. ## NT Fulfillment - Hebrews 3:16-19: They could not enter because of unbelief. - Hebrews 11:30-31: Faith brought down Jericho. - Luke 10:1: Two witnesses s…

from.title: Empty Tomb
from.body: # Empty Tomb ## NT Text — Matt 28:6; John 20:6-7 The tomb is empty; grave-clothes remain. The body is raised, not stolen. ## OT Antecedents - **Ps 16:10** — You will not abandon my soul to Sheol. - **Hos 6:2** — Third day he will raise us. - **Isa 53:10-11** — He shall see his offspring. ## Shared structure - Tomb opened - Linens left - No body - Angelic announcement ## Theological motif Three-day…

to.title: Psalm 16 Holy One Not Decay
to.body: # Psalm 16 / 'You Will Not Let Your Holy One See Decay' → Resurrection Predicted ## OT Story — Psalm 16 David sings: 'I have set the LORD always before me… you will not abandon my soul to Sheol or let your holy one see corruption. You will show me the path of life.' ## NT Fulfillment - Acts 2:25-32: Peter — David died and was buried; he prophesied Christ's resurrection. - Acts 13:35-37: Paul — the…

from.title: Feast Throughout Scripture
from.body: # Feast Throughout Scripture ## NT Text — Matt 22; Luke 14; Rev 19 From Passover to Sinai's covenant meal, to Isaiah 25's feast on the mountain, to the Last Supper, to the marriage supper — feasts are eschatological signs. ## OT Antecedents - **Exod 12** — Passover meal. - **Exod 24:9-11** — Covenant meal on Sinai. - **Isa 25:6** — Feast of fat things on this mountain. ## Shared structure - Shared…

to.title: Mountain Throughout Scripture
to.body: # Mountain Throughout Scripture ## NT Text — Heb 12; Matt 5; Rev 21 Mountains structure the canon: Eden's high garden, Moriah, Sinai, Zion, the Mount of Transfiguration, Mount of Olives, the heavenly Mount Zion. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 22** — Moriah. - **Exod 19** — Sinai. - **2 Sam 5:7** — Zion. - **Ezek 28:14** — Holy mountain of God. ## Shared structure - Ascent - Theophany - Covenant - Sacri…

from.title: ben
from.body: # ben (בֵּן) — son ## Semantic field Son — biological or by adoption. Also forms titles and category-membership ("son of man" = human; "sons of the prophets" = prophetic guild). ## Theological weight Israel is YHWH's son; the Davidic king is begotten Son (Ps 2). Isaac is Abraham's "only son," prefiguring Christ. Sonship undergirds the doctrines of Christ's eternal Sonship and our adoption. ## Sept…

to.title: huios
to.body: # huios (υἱός) — son ## Semantic field Son — biological, adopted, or category-membership ("sons of light"). Translates Hebrew ben. ## Theological weight Jesus is *the* Huios — God's unique Son. Believers become huioi through adoption. The category bears the weight of Davidic-messianic kingship, eternal generation, and adopted family. ## Septuagint mapping Renders Hebrew ben. ## Embedding hint Son …

to.body: # Leah and Rachel / Two Brides → Israel and the Church Made One ## OT Story — Genesis 29:15-30 Jacob loves Rachel but is deceived into marrying Leah first. Both bear sons to him; together with the handmaids they produce the twelve tribes. ## NT Fulfillment - Ephesians 5:25-32: Christ loves one bride, the church. - Galatians 3:28: One people in Christ from Jew and Gentile. - Ruth 4:11: 'Rachel and …

from.title: Absalom Rebellion
from.body: # Absalom's Rebellion → Son Rebels Against Father; Cross Subverts ## OT Story — 2 Samuel 15-18 Absalom steals the people's hearts, drives David from Jerusalem. David crosses the Kidron weeping, ascending the Mount of Olives. Eventually Absalom dies hanging in a tree. ## NT Fulfillment - Luke 19:14: 'We do not want this man to reign over us.' - John 18:1: Jesus crossed the Kidron — same path as Dav…

to.title: Garden Of Gethsemane As Eden Reversed
to.body: # Garden of Gethsemane as Eden Reversed ## NT Text — Matt 26:36-46; John 18:1 Jesus enters a garden to face the will of his Father. Where Adam in the garden chose his own will and brought death, the last Adam chooses the Father's will and accepts death to bring life. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 2-3** — Eden — the garden of disobedience. - **Gen 3:17-19** — Toil and sweat as curse. - **2 Sam 15:23** …

from.title: Quail Provided
from.body: # Quail in the Wilderness → Warning Against Craving ## OT Story — Exodus 16:13; Numbers 11:31-34 God provides quail twice — first as gift alongside manna, later in judgment when Israel craves meat. The graves of craving (Kibroth Hattaavah) mark the second. ## NT Fulfillment - 1 Corinthians 10:6: These things happened as examples that we might not desire evil. - Philippians 3:19: Their god is their…

to.title: Temptation By Stones
to.body: # Temptation: Stones to Bread ## NT Text — Matt 4:3-4; Luke 4:3-4 Satan tempts Jesus to turn stones to bread; Jesus answers from Deuteronomy 8:3, the verse Moses used to interpret the manna. Where Israel grumbled for bread, the Son trusts the word. ## OT Antecedents - **Deut 8:3** — Man does not live by bread alone — Jesus' direct citation. - **Exod 16** — Manna in the wilderness. - **Num 11** — I…

from.title: 2 Cor 5 Reconciliation Through Christ
from.body: # 2 Cor 5: Reconciliation through Christ ## NT Text — 2 Cor 5:18-21 God was in Christ reconciling the world to himself, not counting trespasses. He made him to be sin for us — the great exchange. ## OT Antecedents - **Lev 16** — Day of Atonement bearing of iniquity. - **Isa 53:5-6** — Iniquity of us all laid on him. - **Hos 6:1** — He has torn that he may heal — reconciliation pattern. ## Shared s…

to.title: Adam Christ Two Heads Romans 5
to.body: # Adam and Christ as Two Heads (Rom 5) ## NT Text — Rom 5:12-21 Paul reads humanity as represented by two federal heads: Adam, by whom sin and death entered, and Christ, by whom righteousness and life come. Two acts, two reigns, two destinies. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 2-3** — Adam's covenant headship and fall. - **Ps 51:5** — Inherited sinfulness. - **Hos 6:7** — Like Adam, they transgressed the …

from.body: # They Will Look on Him Whom They Pierced → John 19; Revelation 1 ## OT Story — Zechariah 12:10 'I will pour out on the house of David a spirit of grace and pleas for mercy, so that when they look on me, on him whom they have pierced, they shall mourn.' ## NT Fulfillment - John 19:34-37: Soldier pierced his side; Scripture fulfilled. - Revelation 1:7: Every eye will see him, even those who pierced…

from.body: # Israel My Firstborn → Christ the Firstborn ## OT Story — Exodus 4:22-23 The LORD's word to Pharaoh: 'Israel is my firstborn son. Let my son go that he may serve me. from.body: # Ezekiel 16 / Faithless Bride → Cleansed and Clothed ## OT Story — Ezekiel 16 Jerusalem is depicted as a newborn unwanted girl, washed and clothed by the LORD, made a queen. Yet he will atone and re-establish the covenant. ## NT Fulfillment - Ephesians 5:25-27: Christ gave himself to cleanse her by washing. - Revelation 19:7-8: The bride has made herself ready, granted fine …

to.title: 2 Cor 5 Became Sin For Us
to.body: # 2 Cor 5:21: Became Sin for Us ## NT Text — 2 Cor 5:21 He who knew no sin was made sin for us, that we might become the righteousness of God in him. The most compressed statement of the great exchange. ## OT Antecedents - **Lev 16:21-22** — Scapegoat bearing iniquity. - **Isa 53:6** — LORD laid on him the iniquity of us all. - **Num 19** — Cleansing through death of clean offering. ## Shared stru…

from.title: Coats Of Skin First Sacrifice
from.body: # Coats of Skin → Garments of Salvation ## OT Story — Genesis 3:21 After the fall, Adam and Eve cover themselves with fig leaves. God himself makes garments of skin and clothes them. Implicit in the act is the death of an animal — the first sacrifice and covering. ## NT Fulfillment - Isaiah 61:10: He has clothed me with the garments of salvation, covered me with the robe of righteousness. - Romans…

Only the high priest enters, once a year, with blood. ## NT Fulfillment - Matthew 27:51: At Christ's death the veil is torn from top to bottom. - Hebrews 10:19-22: We enter the holy places by the blood of Jesus, by a new and living way opened thro…

from.title: Psalm 45 Wedding Of King
from.body: # Psalm 45 / Royal Wedding → Marriage of the Lamb ## OT Story — Psalm 45 A royal wedding song. The king's throne is forever; his garments are fragrant; the bride is beautiful in her chambers. 'Forget your people; the king will desire your beauty.' ## NT Fulfillment - Hebrews 1:8-9: 'Your throne, O God' applied directly to the Son. - Ephesians 5:25-32: The mystery of marriage refers to Christ and t…

to.body: # Zephaniah 3 / The King in Your Midst → Christ Dwelling, Singing Over Us ## OT Story — Zephaniah 3:14-17 'Sing aloud, O daughter of Zion! from.title: Hebrews 1 Heir Of All Things
from.body: # Hebrews 1: Heir of All Things ## NT Text — Heb 1:1-4 God has spoken in these last days by his Son, whom he appointed heir of all things, through whom also he made the world. Christ as final word and inheritor. ## OT Antecedents - **Ps 2:8** — Ask of me, and I will give you the nations. - **Prov 8:22-31** — Wisdom present at creation. - **Deut 32:8-9** — Inheritance allotted. ## Shared structure …

to.title: Psalm 2 Anointed King
to.body: # Psalm 2 / 'You Are My Son' → Messiah Enthroned Despite Nations' Rage ## OT Story — Psalm 2 Nations rage and kings plot against the LORD and his anointed. Ask of me, and I will give the nations as your inheritance.' ## NT Fulfillment - Acts 4:25-28: Quoted as fulfilled in the plot against Jesus. - Acts 13:3…

from.body: # Bones of Joseph Carried Out → Hope of Resurrection ## OT Story — Genesis 50:24-26 Dying in Egypt, Joseph commands: 'God will surely visit you; carry up my bones from here.' Moses takes the bones at the exodus; Joshua buries them in the land. ## NT Fulfillment - Hebrews 11:22: By faith Joseph spoke of the exodus and gave instructions concerning his bones. - Ezekiel 37: Dry bones live by the Spiri…

from.title: 1 Peter 5 Chief Shepherd
from.body: # 1 Peter 5: Chief Shepherd ## NT Text — 1 Pet 5:1-4 Elders shepherd the flock; when the Chief Shepherd appears, they will receive the unfading crown of glory. Davidic-shepherd typology gathered into Christ. ## OT Antecedents - **Ezek 34:23** — My servant David — one shepherd. - **Ps 23** — The LORD is my shepherd. - **Zech 11:7-17** — Two shepherd-staffs. ## Shared structure - Under-shepherds - C…

to.title: Restoration Of Peter Three Times
to.body: # Threefold Restoration of Peter ## NT Text — John 21:15-19 By a charcoal fire Jesus asks three times 'do you love me' — undoing three denials and recommissioning Peter to shepherd. ## OT Antecedents - **2 Sam 12** — Nathan-David restoration. - **Ezek 34** — True shepherd. - **Jer 3:12-14** — Return, faithless one. ## Shared structure - Threefold question - Charcoal-fire setting - Recommission - F…

from.title: 1 Peter 1 Born Anew
from.body: # 1 Peter 1: Born Anew through Living Word ## NT Text — 1 Pet 1:22-25 Born again through the living and abiding word of God — Isaiah's 'grass withers, the word stands forever' cited as charter. ## OT Antecedents - **Isa 40:6-8** — Grass withers, word stands forever. - **Ezek 36:26** — New heart and Spirit. - **Ps 119:25** — Give me life according to your word. ## Shared structure - New birth - Liv…

to.title: 2 Peter 1 Make Calling Sure
to.body: # 2 Peter 1: Make Your Calling Sure ## NT Text — 2 Pet 1:3-11 Add to your faith virtue, knowledge, self-control, perseverance, godliness, brotherly affection, love. By these qualities you confirm your calling and election. ## OT Antecedents - **Prov 1:7** — Beginning of knowledge. - **Ps 15** — Who shall dwell on your holy hill? - **Mic 6:8** — Walk humbly with God. ## Shared structure - Faith as …

from.title: Psalm 22 Crucifixion
from.body: # Psalm 22 → The Crucifixion ## OT Story — Psalm 22 A Davidic lament that pivots from extreme suffering to vindicated praise. The opening cry — *"My God, my God, why have you forsaken me?"* (22:1) — gives way to a sequence of concrete tortures: - *"All who see me mock me; they make mouths at me, they wag their heads"* (22:7) - *"He trusts in the LORD; let him deliver him!"* (22:8) — the mockers' t…

to.title: Kiss Of Judas
to.body: # Kiss of Judas ## NT Text — Matt 26:47-50; Luke 22:47-48 Judas betrays Jesus with a kiss. The gesture inverts covenant intimacy into treachery, echoing Joab's kiss-and-kill of Amasa and the proverb that an enemy's kisses are deceitful. ## OT Antecedents - **2 Sam 20:9-10** — Joab kisses Amasa and stabs him. - **Ps 41:9** — My close friend who ate my bread lifted his heel — cited in John 13:18. - …

from.title: 1 Peter 2 Royal Priesthood

to.title: Hebrews 12 Mount Zion Not Sinai
to.body: # Hebrews 12: Mount Zion, Not Sinai ## NT Text — Heb 12:18-24 You have not come to Sinai with its terror but to Mount Zion, to the heavenly Jerusalem, to angels, to the assembly of the firstborn, to Jesus the mediator. ## OT Antecedents - **Exod 19** — Sinai theophany of terror. - **Ps 48** — Zion's beauty. - **Isa 60** — Zion glorified. ## Shared structure - Sinai/terror contrast - Zion/grace des…

from.title: Manna In Wilderness

to.title: Pillar Of Cloud And Fire
to.body: # Pillar of Cloud and Fire → God Tabernacling With Us ## OT Story — Exodus 13:21-22; 40:34-38 The LORD goes before Israel as a pillar of cloud by day and fire by night. from.title: Cleansing Of Temple
