to.title: Veil Of Temple Torn
to.body: # Veil of the Temple Torn ## NT Text — Matt 27:51 At Christ's death the temple veil tears top to bottom. The barrier between holy and most-holy is rent. ## OT Antecedents - **Exod 26:31-33** — Veil separating holy from most-holy. - **Lev 16:2** — Aaron enters only on Day of Atonement. - **Isa 25:7** — He will destroy the covering. ## Shared structure - Top-down tearing - Sanctuary access - End of …

from.title: 1 Peter 2 He Bore Our Sins
from.body: # 1 Peter 2: He Bore Our Sins ## NT Text — 1 Pet 2:21-25 Christ left an example; he himself bore our sins in his body on the tree, that we might die to sin and live to righteousness. Isaiah 53 is paraphrased throughout. ## OT Antecedents - **Isa 53:4-6** — He bore our griefs; by his wounds we are healed. - **Isa 53:7** — Silent like a lamb. - **Isa 53:11** — Bear their iniquities. ## Shared struct…

to.title: Psalm 69 Reproaches Fell On Me
to.body: # Psalm 69 / 'Zeal for Your House Has Consumed Me' → Christ's Passion ## OT Story — Psalm 69 The psalmist drowns in deep waters; reproach has broken his heart; they gave him vinegar to drink; zeal for God's house consumes him. ## NT Fulfillment - John 2:17: 'Zeal for your house will consume me' at the temple cleansing. - John 15:25: 'They hated me without cause.' - Romans 15:3: The reproaches of t…

from.title: Isaiah 53 Suffering Servant
from.body: # Isaiah 53 Suffering Servant → Christ's Passion ## OT Story — Isaiah 52:13-53:12 The fourth and climactic Servant Song. The Servant is *"despised and rejected"* (53:3), *"pierced for our transgressions, crushed for our iniquities"* (53:5). He is silent before his accusers — *"like a lamb that is led to the slaughter, and like a sheep that before its shearers is silent, so he opened not his mouth"…

to.title: Wisdom Of Solomon Suffering Righteous
to.body: # Wisdom 2 / The Righteous Mocked → Mockery at the Cross ## OT Story — Wisdom of Solomon 2:12-20 'Let us lie in wait for the righteous man, because he is troublesome to us… He calls himself a child of the Lord. Let us see if his words are true… Let us test him… condemn him to a shameful death.' ## NT Fulfillment - Matthew 27:39-43: 'He trusts in God; let God deliver him… He said, I am the Son of G…

from.title: Lamb Throughout Scripture
from.body: # Lamb Throughout Scripture ## NT Text — John 1:29; Rev 5 Abel's firstborn, Abraham's substitute, the Passover lamb, the daily tamid, Isaiah's silent lamb, John's 'Lamb of God', Revelation's slain Lamb. The lamb is canon's central animal. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 4:4** — Abel's firstborn lamb. - **Gen 22:8** — God will provide himself a lamb. - **Exod 12** — Passover lamb. - **Isa 53:7** — Like a…

to.title: Serpent Throughout Scripture
to.body: # Serpent Throughout Scripture ## NT Text — John 3:14; Rev 12, 20 The serpent of Eden, the bronze serpent in the wilderness, the dragons of Daniel and the Psalms, the dragon-serpent of Revelation finally bound. The serpent's head is crushed. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 3:1-15** — Serpent in the garden. - **Num 21:8-9** — Bronze serpent lifted up. - **Ps 74:13-14** — Leviathan crushed. - **Isa 27:1**…

from.title: Healing At Beautiful Gate
from.body: # Healing at the Beautiful Gate ## What happens Peter and John, going up to the temple, command a lame man at the Beautiful Gate to walk in the name of Jesus; he leaps and enters the temple praising God. ## Typological significance Isaiah 35's 'the lame shall leap' fulfilled at the very threshold of the temple — the kingdom invading the old sanctuary. Jesus' authority continues through His apostle…

to.title: Healing Of Man Born Blind
to.body: # Healing of the Man Born Blind ## What happens Jesus mixes clay with saliva, anoints the eyes of a man blind from birth, and sends him to wash in the pool of Siloam ('Sent'). He returns seeing, and confesses Jesus as Lord. ## Typological significance Re-creation imagery: clay from the ground, divine breath, Sabbath-day formation of a new humanity. The progressive illumination of the man contrasts…

from.title: Romans 8 No Condemnation
from.body: # Romans 8: No Condemnation ## NT Text — Rom 8:1-39 Paul declares no condemnation for those in Christ Jesus. The chapter weaves Spirit-life, adoption, suffering, and unbreakable love. ## OT Antecedents - **Isa 50:8-9** — Who is my adversary? Let him come near. - **Ps 44:22** — For your sake we are killed all day long — quoted. - **Hos 11** — Father's love for prodigal son. ## Shared structure - Fo…

to.title: Romans 9 Potter And Clay
to.body: # Romans 9: Potter and Clay ## NT Text — Rom 9:19-24 Paul defends divine election with the potter-and-clay image. God's freedom is upheld; mercy is glory. ## OT Antecedents - **Isa 29:16; 45:9** — Potter and clay. - **Jer 18:1-11** — Potter's house. - **Job 38:4** — Where were you when I laid earth's foundations? ## Shared structure - Potter sovereignty - Clay reciprocal silence - Vessels of mercy…

from.title: 2 Cor 5 Reconciliation Through Christ
from.body: # 2 Cor 5: Reconciliation through Christ ## NT Text — 2 Cor 5:18-21 God was in Christ reconciling the world to himself, not counting trespasses. He made him to be sin for us — the great exchange. ## OT Antecedents - **Lev 16** — Day of Atonement bearing of iniquity. - **Isa 53:5-6** — Iniquity of us all laid on him. - **Hos 6:1** — He has torn that he may heal — reconciliation pattern. ## Shared s…

to.title: Veil Of Temple Torn
to.body: # Veil of the Temple Torn ## NT Text — Matt 27:51 At Christ's death the temple veil tears top to bottom. The barrier between holy and most-holy is rent. ## OT Antecedents - **Exod 26:31-33** — Veil separating holy from most-holy. - **Lev 16:2** — Aaron enters only on Day of Atonement. - **Isa 25:7** — He will destroy the covering. ## Shared structure - Top-down tearing - Sanctuary access - End of …

from.title: 1 Cor 10 Rock Was Christ
from.body: # 1 Cor 10: The Rock Was Christ ## NT Text — 1 Cor 10:1-13 Paul reads Israel's wilderness experiences typologically: cloud, sea, spiritual food and drink — 'and the Rock was Christ.' Sacrament-shaped warning. ## OT Antecedents - **Exod 14** — Cloud and sea. - **Exod 17** — Water from the rock. - **Num 20** — Second rock-incident. ## Shared structure - Cloud/sea as baptism - Manna as spiritual food…

to.title: Colossians 1 Cosmic Christ
to.body: # Colossians 1: Cosmic Christ ## NT Text — Col 1:15-20 Christ is the image of God, firstborn over creation; in him all things hold together. The hymn weaves Genesis, Proverbs 8 wisdom, and reconciliation. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 1** — Creator of all things. - **Prov 8:22-31** — Wisdom present at creation. - **Ps 89:27** — Firstborn, highest of kings. ## Shared structure - Image of God - Pre-exis…

from.title: Sevenfold Spirit
from.body: # Sevenfold Spirit on the Branch → Sevenfold Spirit Before the Throne ## OT Story — Isaiah 11:1-2 On the branch from Jesse's stump the Spirit of the LORD rests: Spirit of wisdom and understanding, counsel and might, knowledge and fear of the LORD — sevenfold listing. ## NT Fulfillment - Revelation 1:4: From the seven spirits before his throne. - Revelation 4:5: Seven torches of fire — the seven sp…

to.title: Spirit Descending As Dove
to.body: # Spirit Descending as a Dove ## NT Text — Matt 3:16; John 1:32 The Holy Spirit descends on Jesus 'like a dove,' evoking the creation-Spirit hovering over the waters and the dove of Noah's ark returning over a new-creation world. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 1:2** — Spirit hovering over the waters — same verb in some Targums uses dove imagery. - **Gen 8:8-12** — Dove returns with olive leaf — new-cre…

from.title: Showbread Twelve Loaves
from.body: # Bread of the Presence / Twelve Loaves → Christ and His Twelve ## OT Story — Exodus 25:30; Leviticus 24:5-9 Twelve loaves sit on a gold table in the holy place 'before the LORD always.' Priests eat them weekly in the holy place. ## NT Fulfillment - John 6:35: I am the bread of life. - 1 Corinthians 10:17: We who are many are one bread, one body. - Mark 2:25-26: David's eating the showbread antici…

to.title: Twelve Apostles Echo Twelve Tribes
to.body: # Twelve Apostles Echo Twelve Tribes ## NT Text — Matt 19:28; Rev 21:14 Jesus promises the Twelve will sit on twelve thrones judging the twelve tribes; Revelation 21 names the foundations of the New Jerusalem after them. Tribal Israel and apostolic Israel meet in the heavenly city. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 49:28** — Twelve tribes of Israel. - **Exod 28:21** — Twelve stones on the high priest's br…

from.title: Daniel 9 Seventy Weeks
from.body: # Seventy Weeks of Daniel → The Time of Messiah ## OT Story — Daniel 9:24-27 Seventy weeks are decreed to finish transgression, atone for iniquity, and bring everlasting righteousness. An anointed one will come and be cut off. ## NT Fulfillment - Galatians 4:4: When the fullness of time had come, God sent his Son. - Luke 24:44: Everything written about me must be fulfilled. - Mark 1:15: The time i…

to.title: Ezekiel Apocrypha Themes
to.body: # 4 Ezra / Apocalyptic Hope → New Creation Theology Backdrop ## OT Story — 2 Esdras (4 Ezra) 7 4 Ezra wrestles with theodicy, exile, and resurrection — featuring the Messiah's reign, new age dawning, and consolation of the righteous. ## NT Fulfillment - Romans 8:18-25: Sufferings of this present time vs. coming glory. - 2 Corinthians 4:17: Light affliction working eternal glory. - Revelation 21:1-…

from.title: Psalm 2 Anointed King
from.body: # Psalm 2 / 'You Are My Son' → Messiah Enthroned Despite Nations' Rage ## OT Story — Psalm 2 Nations rage and kings plot against the LORD and his anointed. The LORD laughs; he installs his King on Zion. 'You are my Son; today I have begotten you. Ask of me, and I will give the nations as your inheritance.' ## NT Fulfillment - Acts 4:25-28: Quoted as fulfilled in the plot against Jesus. - Acts 13:3…

to.title: Voice From Heaven Beloved Son
to.body: # Voice from Heaven: Beloved Son ## NT Text — Matt 3:17; 17:5 At baptism and transfiguration the Father declares, 'This is my beloved Son.' The formula fuses Psalm 2's royal coronation with Isaiah 42's Servant and possibly Genesis 22's 'only/beloved son.' ## OT Antecedents - **Ps 2:7** — Royal son begotten today. - **Isa 42:1** — Chosen in whom my soul delights. - **Gen 22:2** — Take your son, you…

from.title: Isaiah 11 Branch Of Jesse
from.body: # Branch from Jesse's Stump → Peaceable Kingdom of the Root ## OT Story — Isaiah 11:1-10 From the stump of Jesse a shoot comes; the Spirit rests on him. He judges with righteousness. The wolf dwells with the lamb. The earth is full of the knowledge of the LORD. ## NT Fulfillment - Romans 15:12: 'The root of Jesse will come; in him the Gentiles will hope.' - Revelation 5:5: The Root of David has co…

to.title: Zephaniah King In Midst
to.body: # Zephaniah 3 / The King in Your Midst → Christ Dwelling, Singing Over Us ## OT Story — Zephaniah 3:14-17 'Sing aloud, O daughter of Zion! The King of Israel, the LORD, is in your midst… He will rejoice over you with gladness; he will quiet you by his love; he will exult over you with loud singing.' ## NT Fulfillment - John 1:14: The Word became flesh and dwelt among us. - Matthew 1:23: God with u…

from.title: Isaiah 35 Highway Of Holiness
from.body: # Highway of Holiness in the Desert → The Way for the Redeemed ## OT Story — Isaiah 35:1-10 The wilderness blossoms; weak hands are strengthened; eyes of the blind opened; the lame leap. A highway is there, called the Way of Holiness — only the redeemed walk it. ## NT Fulfillment - Matthew 11:4-6: Jesus answers John — the blind see, the lame walk… (Isa 35 echo). - John 14:6: I am the way. - Acts 9…

to.title: Road To Emmaus
to.body: # Road to Emmaus ## NT Text — Luke 24:13-32 The risen Christ walks with two disciples and opens 'Moses and all the prophets' to show the necessity of messianic suffering. ## OT Antecedents - **Deut 18:15** — Prophet like Moses. - **Isa 53** — Suffering Servant. - **Ps 22; Ps 16** — Suffering and resurrection psalms. ## Shared structure - Walking journey - Stranger reveals Scripture - Hearts burnin…

from.title: Jonah Three Days
from.body: # Jonah Three Days in the Fish → Three Days in the Tomb This typology is **the only one Jesus himself names explicitly as a sign**. It is therefore exceptionally load-bearing — if any typology is methodologically validated by canonical scripture, this one is. ## OT Story — Jonah 1-2 God calls Jonah to preach repentance to Nineveh, the capital of Israel's enemy Assyria. Jonah flees by ship in the o…

to.title: Joseph Sold By Brothers
to.body: # Joseph Sold by His Brothers → The Betrayal and Vindication of Christ ## OT Story — Genesis 37, 40, 41-50 Joseph is the **beloved son** of Jacob, given a special garment (the "coat of many colors"). He has prophetic dreams in which his brothers and parents bow down to him. His brothers hate him and plot to kill him; instead, on Judah's suggestion, they sell him for **twenty pieces of silver** to …

from.title: Revelation 12 Woman Crowned Stars
from.body: # Revelation 12: Woman Crowned with Stars ## NT Text — Rev 12:1-6 A woman clothed with the sun, crown of twelve stars, gives birth to a male child. Joseph's dream of celestial homage rises again, and Israel/the Messianic community is figured. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 37:9-11** — Joseph's sun-moon-stars dream. - **Isa 66:7-9** — Zion's labor and birth. - **Mic 5:3** — Until she who is in travail b…

to.title: Words From Cross Woman Behold Son
to.body: # Word from Cross: Woman, Behold Your Son ## NT Text — John 19:26-27 Jesus entrusts his mother to the beloved disciple. The new-covenant family is constituted at the cross. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 3:15** — Woman whose seed crushes serpent. - **Ps 22:9-10** — Mother who bore me. - **Ruth 4:14-17** — Naomi receives son via kinsman-redemption. ## Shared structure - Mother and son - New familial bon…

from.title: Manna Bread Of Life
from.body: # The Manna in the Wilderness → Christ the Bread of Life The clearest "bread typology" in scripture — and **Jesus develops it himself at length** in John 6. ## OT Story — Exodus 16 Israel in the wilderness complains of hunger. God sends: - **Bread from heaven** — appears every morning with the dew - **Each gathers an omer per person** — equal portions, none left over - **Cannot be stored** — excep…

to.title: Feast Throughout Scripture
to.body: # Feast Throughout Scripture ## NT Text — Matt 22; Luke 14; Rev 19 From Passover to Sinai's covenant meal, to Isaiah 25's feast on the mountain, to the Last Supper, to the marriage supper — feasts are eschatological signs. ## OT Antecedents - **Exod 12** — Passover meal. - **Exod 24:9-11** — Covenant meal on Sinai. - **Isa 25:6** — Feast of fat things on this mountain. ## Shared structure - Shared…

from.title: Hebrews 11 Cloud Of Witnesses
from.body: # Hebrews 11: Cloud of Witnesses ## NT Text — Heb 11:1-12:1 A roll-call of faith — Abel, Enoch, Noah, Abraham, Sarah, Moses, Rahab, and more — culminates in 'such a great cloud of witnesses.' Faith looks forward to the same promise we possess. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 4-25** — Patriarchal exemplars. - **Exod** — Mosaic figures. - **2 Kgs 2** — Elijah taken up. ## Shared structure - Definition of …

to.title: Hebrews 12 Mount Zion Not Sinai
to.body: # Hebrews 12: Mount Zion, Not Sinai ## NT Text — Heb 12:18-24 You have not come to Sinai with its terror but to Mount Zion, to the heavenly Jerusalem, to angels, to the assembly of the firstborn, to Jesus the mediator. ## OT Antecedents - **Exod 19** — Sinai theophany of terror. - **Ps 48** — Zion's beauty. - **Isa 60** — Zion glorified. ## Shared structure - Sinai/terror contrast - Zion/grace des…

from.title: Revelation 13 Beast From Sea
from.body: # Revelation 13: Beast from the Sea ## NT Text — Rev 13:1-10 A beast rises from the sea — Daniel 7's four beasts combined into one. Political tyranny absorbs the world's worship. ## OT Antecedents - **Dan 7:2-7** — Four beasts from the sea. - **Ps 74:13-14** — Heads of leviathan crushed. - **Isa 27:1** — Leviathan, the twisting serpent. ## Shared structure - Sea origin - Many heads/horns - Blasphe…

to.title: Revelation 16 Bowls Of Wrath
to.body: # Revelation 16: Bowls of Wrath ## NT Text — Rev 16 Seven bowls of wrath are poured — sores, blood, scorching sun, darkness, Euphrates dried. Egyptian plagues climax in cosmic recapitulation. ## OT Antecedents - **Exod 7-12** — Egyptian plagues. - **Isa 51:17** — Cup of wrath drained. - **Jer 25:15-29** — Cup of wrath given to nations. ## Shared structure - Bowls poured - Seven completes - Plague-…

from.title: Gibeonite Deception
from.body: # Gibeonite Deception → Discernment and Covenant Faithfulness ## OT Story — Joshua 9 The Gibeonites dress in worn clothing and pretend to be from afar. Israel makes a covenant without asking the LORD. When the deceit is discovered, the covenant stands; they become servants in God's house. ## NT Fulfillment - Matthew 10:16: Wise as serpents, innocent as doves. - 2 Corinthians 11:14: Satan disguises…

to.title: Gideons Fleece
to.body: # Gideon's Fleece → Signs and Faith ## OT Story — Judges 6:36-40 Gideon asks God for confirmation: dew on the fleece but not the ground; then dew on the ground but not the fleece. God grants both signs. ## NT Fulfillment - John 20:24-29: Thomas requires a sign; Jesus rebukes yet grants — blessed are those who believe without seeing. - James 1:6: Let him ask in faith, with no doubting. - Matthew 12…

from.title: Road To Emmaus
from.body: # Road to Emmaus ## NT Text — Luke 24:13-32 The risen Christ walks with two disciples and opens 'Moses and all the prophets' to show the necessity of messianic suffering. ## OT Antecedents - **Deut 18:15** — Prophet like Moses. - **Isa 53** — Suffering Servant. - **Ps 22; Ps 16** — Suffering and resurrection psalms. ## Shared structure - Walking journey - Stranger reveals Scripture - Hearts burnin…

to.title: Tobit Healing Journey
to.body: # Tobit's Journey / Angel's Healing → Christ as Companion-Healer ## OT Story — Tobit 1-14 Tobit, blind and grieving, sends his son Tobias on a journey. The angel Raphael accompanies them disguised; Tobias is delivered from a demon, marries Sarah, returns to heal his father. ## NT Fulfillment - Hebrews 13:2: Some have entertained angels unawares. - Luke 10:25-37: The Good Samaritan binds wounds. - …

from.title: Bronze Serpent
from.body: # The Bronze Serpent → "The Son of Man Must Be Lifted Up" One of the typologies **Jesus himself names explicitly**. As with Jonah (Mt 12:40), Jesus' own use of this OT story authorizes its typological reading and tells us exactly how he wants it understood. ## OT Story — Numbers 21:4-9 Israel grumbles against God during the wilderness journey. God sends "fiery serpents" whose bite kills many. The …

to.title: Rock Gives Water
to.body: # Rock Gives Water → "The Rock Was Christ" (1 Cor 10:4) ## OT Story — Exodus 17 & Numbers 20 **Exodus 17:1-7** — Israel camps at Rephidim. There is no water. The people quarrel with Moses and *"tested the LORD by saying, 'Is the LORD among us or not?'"* (17:7). God commands Moses: *"Behold, I will stand before you there on **the rock at Horeb**, and you shall **strike the rock**, and water shall c…

from.title: Psalm 22 Crucifixion
from.body: # Psalm 22 → The Crucifixion ## OT Story — Psalm 22 A Davidic lament that pivots from extreme suffering to vindicated praise. The opening cry — *"My God, my God, why have you forsaken me?"* (22:1) — gives way to a sequence of concrete tortures: - *"All who see me mock me; they make mouths at me, they wag their heads"* (22:7) - *"He trusts in the LORD; let him deliver him!"* (22:8) — the mockers' t…

to.title: Stephens Martyrdom Like Christ
to.body: # Stephen's Martyrdom Patterned on Christ ## NT Text — Acts 7:54-60 Stephen prays 'receive my spirit' and 'do not hold this sin against them' — directly echoing the cross-words of Jesus. The first Christian martyr dies like his Lord. ## OT Antecedents - **Ps 31:5** — Into your hand I commit my spirit. - **Isa 53:12** — Intercession for transgressors. - **2 Chr 24:20-22** — Zechariah son of Jehoiad…

from.title: Hanukkah Light Returns
from.body: # Hanukkah / Rededication of the Temple → Christ Speaks at the Feast ## OT Story — 1 Maccabees 4:36-59 After Antiochus' desecration, the Maccabees cleanse and rededicate the temple. The Feast of Dedication (Hanukkah) commemorates the return of light. ## NT Fulfillment - John 10:22-30: At the Feast of Dedication Jesus declares his unity with the Father. - John 8:12: I am the light of the world. - H…

to.title: Dedication Of Temple
to.body: # Dedication of the Temple ## What it is The inaugural festival at the consecration of the temple — first under Solomon, then in the second-temple rededication under Ezra, and again under the Maccabees — when the glory of YHWH filled the sanctuary. ## Key elements - Massive sacrificial offerings - Solomon's intercessory prayer - Glory cloud filling the house - Fourteen days of feasting (1 Kgs 8) -…

from.title: 1 Cor 10 Rock Was Christ
from.body: # 1 Cor 10: The Rock Was Christ ## NT Text — 1 Cor 10:1-13 Paul reads Israel's wilderness experiences typologically: cloud, sea, spiritual food and drink — 'and the Rock was Christ.' Sacrament-shaped warning. ## OT Antecedents - **Exod 14** — Cloud and sea. - **Exod 17** — Water from the rock. - **Num 20** — Second rock-incident. ## Shared structure - Cloud/sea as baptism - Manna as spiritual food…

to.title: Forty Years Wilderness
to.body: # Forty Years in Wilderness → Christ's Forty Days ## OT Story — Numbers 14:33-34 Israel is tested forty years in the wilderness — humbled, fed with manna, made to know that man does not live by bread alone (Deut 8:3). ## NT Fulfillment - Matthew 4:1-11: Jesus tested forty days in the wilderness — quotes Deuteronomy 8. - Hebrews 3:7-19: They could not enter because of unbelief. - Hebrews 4:1-11: A …

from.title: Vine Throughout Scripture
from.body: # Vine Throughout Scripture ## NT Text — John 15 Israel as vine in Psalm 80 and Isaiah 5 — disappointing fruit. Christ as the True Vine, with believers as branches abiding in him. ## OT Antecedents - **Ps 80:8-16** — Vine brought out of Egypt. - **Isa 5:1-7** — Vineyard of the LORD. - **Jer 2:21** — Choice vine turned degenerate. - **Hos 10:1** — Israel a luxuriant vine. ## Shared structure - Plan…

to.title: Ezekiels Eagles And Vine
to.body: # Ezekiel's Eagles and Vine ## What happens Ezekiel describes two great eagles and a vine: one eagle plants the vine, which then turns toward another; God will plant a tender twig that grows into a noble cedar. ## Typological significance Geopolitical allegory of Judah caught between Babylon and Egypt, ending in a messianic promise: the tender twig planted by God on the high mountain prefigures Ch…

from.title: Isaiah 11 Branch Of Jesse
from.body: # Branch from Jesse's Stump → Peaceable Kingdom of the Root ## OT Story — Isaiah 11:1-10 From the stump of Jesse a shoot comes; the Spirit rests on him. He judges with righteousness. The wolf dwells with the lamb. The earth is full of the knowledge of the LORD. ## NT Fulfillment - Romans 15:12: 'The root of Jesse will come; in him the Gentiles will hope.' - Revelation 5:5: The Root of David has co…

to.title: Psalm 72 Royal Reign
to.body: # Psalm 72 / Solomon's Reign → Messiah's Universal Just Reign ## OT Story — Psalm 72 A psalm of Solomon: he judges with righteousness, defends the poor, kings of Sheba bring gifts, all nations are blessed in him, his name endures forever. ## NT Fulfillment - Matthew 2:11: Magi bring gold, frankincense, myrrh — echo of Sheba. - Revelation 21:24-26: Kings bring glory into the city. - Galatians 3:14:…

from.title: Jacobs Ladder
from.body: # Jacob's Ladder → Jesus as the Ladder (His Own Citation) ## OT Story — Genesis 28:10-22 Jacob is fleeing his brother Esau, who wants to kill him for the stolen blessing. Alone, in the open, he stops *"because the sun had set"*. He takes a stone for a pillow and lies down. He dreams: *"behold, a **ladder** [*sullam* — more accurately a stairway/ramp] set up on the earth, and the **top of it reache…

to.title: Joseph In The Pit
to.body: # Joseph in the Pit → Descent and Deliverance ## OT Story — Genesis 37:18-24 The brothers strip Joseph of his ornate robe and cast him into an empty cistern in the wilderness. From the pit he is drawn up and sold into Egypt. ## NT Fulfillment - Psalm 88:6: 'You have put me in the depths of the pit' — lament that anticipates Christ's descent. - Acts 2:24: Death could not hold him. - Ephesians 4:9-1…

from.title: Leah Rachel Two Brides
from.body: # Leah and Rachel / Two Brides → Israel and the Church Made One ## OT Story — Genesis 29:15-30 Jacob loves Rachel but is deceived into marrying Leah first. Both bear sons to him; together with the handmaids they produce the twelve tribes. ## NT Fulfillment - Ephesians 5:25-32: Christ loves one bride, the church. - Galatians 3:28: One people in Christ from Jew and Gentile. - Ruth 4:11: 'Rachel and …

to.title: Wedding Throughout Scripture
to.body: # Wedding Throughout Scripture ## NT Text — John 2; Rev 19 From Adam's awakening to Eve to Cana's water-turned-wine to the marriage supper of the Lamb, weddings mark new-creation thresholds. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 2:22-24** — First wedding. - **Song 3:11** — Day of Solomon's wedding. - **Isa 62:5** — Like a bridegroom rejoices. ## Shared structure - Anticipation - Feast - Joy - Covenant initiat…

from.title: Ezekiels Eagles And Vine
from.body: # Ezekiel's Eagles and Vine ## What happens Ezekiel describes two great eagles and a vine: one eagle plants the vine, which then turns toward another; God will plant a tender twig that grows into a noble cedar. ## Typological significance Geopolitical allegory of Judah caught between Babylon and Egypt, ending in a messianic promise: the tender twig planted by God on the high mountain prefigures Ch…

to.title: True Vine Discourse
to.body: # The True Vine Discourse ## What happens Jesus declares 'I am the true vine, my Father is the vinedresser.' Branches abiding in Him bear much fruit; those that do not are pruned away and burned. ## Typological significance Israel as failed vine (Psalm 80, Isaiah 5) replaced by Christ as the true vine — believers as branches in Him. The vine metaphor reframes covenant union as organic indwelling. …

from.title: 1 Peter 1 Living Hope
from.body: # 1 Peter 1: Living Hope ## NT Text — 1 Pet 1:3-9 Peter blesses God for a living hope through the resurrection of Jesus Christ. The inheritance is imperishable, undefiled, unfading, kept in heaven. ## OT Antecedents - **Num 18:20** — LORD as inheritance. - **Ps 16:5-6** — The LORD is my chosen portion. - **Dan 12:2-3** — Resurrection inheritance. ## Shared structure - New birth - Living hope - Imp…

to.title: James 1 Tested Faith
to.body: # James 1: Tested Faith ## NT Text — James 1:2-12 Count it all joy when you meet trials, for the testing of faith produces steadfastness. Trial pattern echoes Abraham's testing and Job's endurance. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 22** — Abraham's testing. - **Job 1-2** — Job's trial. - **Prov 17:3** — Crucible for silver and furnace for gold. ## Shared structure - Trial encountered - Steadfastness produ…

from.title: 1 Cor 5 Christ Our Passover
from.body: # 1 Cor 5: Christ Our Passover ## NT Text — 1 Cor 5:7-8 Paul calls Christ 'our Passover lamb, sacrificed for us,' and exhorts cleansing of old leaven. Passover and Unleavened Bread become ethical patterns. ## OT Antecedents - **Exod 12** — Passover ordinance. - **Exod 13:6-7** — Unleavened bread for seven days. - **Deut 16:1-8** — Passover-Unleavened Bread combined. ## Shared structure - Lamb slai…

to.title: Titus 2 Blessed Hope
to.body: # Titus 2: Blessed Hope ## NT Text — Titus 2:11-14 Grace appears teaching us to live godly, awaiting the blessed hope — the appearing of the glory of our great God and Savior Jesus Christ. ## OT Antecedents - **Ps 67** — God's saving way among all nations. - **Isa 40:5** — Glory of the LORD revealed. - **Mal 3:1** — Lord whom you seek will come. ## Shared structure - Grace appeared (incarnation) -…

from.title: Vine Throughout Scripture
from.body: # Vine Throughout Scripture ## NT Text — John 15 Israel as vine in Psalm 80 and Isaiah 5 — disappointing fruit. Christ as the True Vine, with believers as branches abiding in him. ## OT Antecedents - **Ps 80:8-16** — Vine brought out of Egypt. - **Isa 5:1-7** — Vineyard of the LORD. - **Jer 2:21** — Choice vine turned degenerate. - **Hos 10:1** — Israel a luxuriant vine. ## Shared structure - Plan…

to.title: Wedding Throughout Scripture
to.body: # Wedding Throughout Scripture ## NT Text — John 2; Rev 19 From Adam's awakening to Eve to Cana's water-turned-wine to the marriage supper of the Lamb, weddings mark new-creation thresholds. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 2:22-24** — First wedding. - **Song 3:11** — Day of Solomon's wedding. - **Isa 62:5** — Like a bridegroom rejoices. ## Shared structure - Anticipation - Feast - Joy - Covenant initiat…

from.title: Judah Tamar Levirate
from.body: # Judah and Tamar / Levirate → Messianic Line Preserved ## OT Story — Genesis 38 Tamar, widowed and denied levirate marriage, disguises herself and conceives twins by Judah. Judah confesses, 'She is more righteous than I.' Perez is born — ancestor of David and Jesus. ## NT Fulfillment - Matthew 1:3: Tamar named in Jesus' genealogy. - Ruth 4:18-22: Perez's line leads to David. - Romans 5:20: Where …

to.title: Rahab Scarlet Cord
to.body: # Rahab's Scarlet Cord → Blood-Marked Household Saved ## OT Story — Joshua 2; 6:22-25 Rahab the Canaanite prostitute hides the spies and asks for mercy. They give her a scarlet cord to hang from her window; only those in her house with the cord will be spared when Jericho falls. ## NT Fulfillment - Hebrews 11:31: By faith Rahab did not perish. - James 2:25: She was justified by works that demonstr…

from.title: 2 Cor 5 Reconciliation Through Christ
from.body: # 2 Cor 5: Reconciliation through Christ ## NT Text — 2 Cor 5:18-21 God was in Christ reconciling the world to himself, not counting trespasses. He made him to be sin for us — the great exchange. ## OT Antecedents - **Lev 16** — Day of Atonement bearing of iniquity. - **Isa 53:5-6** — Iniquity of us all laid on him. - **Hos 6:1** — He has torn that he may heal — reconciliation pattern. ## Shared s…

to.title: Romans 6 Baptized Into Death
to.body: # Romans 6: Baptized into His Death ## NT Text — Rom 6:1-11 Believers are baptized into Christ's death and raised to walk in newness of life. Baptism is union with the Death-and-Resurrection of Christ. ## OT Antecedents - **Exod 14** — Red Sea passage as death/resurrection of Israel. - **Josh 3** — Jordan crossing into the land. - **Ezek 36:25-27** — Sprinkled with clean water; new spirit. ## Shar…

from.title: Carrying Of Cross Like Isaac
from.body: # Carrying the Cross like Isaac ## NT Text — John 19:17 Jesus carries his own cross to Calvary, evoking Isaac carrying the wood up Moriah — beloved son bearing the means of his own death. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 22:6** — Isaac carries the wood. - **Isa 53:7** — Silent like a lamb. - **Num 19** — Heifer outside the camp. ## Shared structure - Beloved son carries wood - Father purposes offering - …

to.title: Hebrews 13 Outside The Camp
to.body: # Hebrews 13: Outside the Camp ## NT Text — Heb 13:11-14 Jesus suffered outside the gate to sanctify the people through his own blood. Let us go to him outside the camp, bearing his reproach. ## OT Antecedents - **Lev 16:27** — Sin-offering carcasses burned outside the camp. - **Num 19:3** — Red heifer slain outside the camp. - **Exod 33:7** — Tent of meeting pitched outside the camp. ## Shared st…

to.title: Road To Emmaus
to.body: # Road to Emmaus ## NT Text — Luke 24:13-32 The risen Christ walks with two disciples and opens 'Moses and all the prophets' to show the necessity of messianic suffering. ## OT Antecedents - **Deut 18:15** — Prophet like Moses. - **Isa 53** — Suffering Servant. - **Ps 22; Ps 16** — Suffering and resurrection psalms. ## Shared structure - Walking journey - Stranger reveals Scripture - Hearts burnin…

from.title: 1 Peter 2 He Bore Our Sins
from.body: # 1 Peter 2: He Bore Our Sins ## NT Text — 1 Pet 2:21-25 Christ left an example; he himself bore our sins in his body on the tree, that we might die to sin and live to righteousness. Isaiah 53 is paraphrased throughout. ## OT Antecedents - **Isa 53:4-6** — He bore our griefs; by his wounds we are healed. - **Isa 53:7** — Silent like a lamb. - **Isa 53:11** — Bear their iniquities. ## Shared struct…

to.title: 1 Peter 2 Royal Priesthood
to.body: # 1 Peter 2: Royal Priesthood ## NT Text — 1 Pet 2:9-10 You are a chosen race, royal priesthood, holy nation, people for his possession — Exodus 19:6 reapplied to the church. ## OT Antecedents - **Exod 19:5-6** — Kingdom of priests, holy nation. - **Hos 1:9-10; 2:23** — Not-my-people now my people. - **Isa 43:21** — People formed for myself. ## Shared structure - Exodus identity titles - Hosea rev…

from.body: # Carrying the Cross like Isaac ## NT Text — John 19:17 Jesus carries his own cross to Calvary, evoking Isaac carrying the wood up Moriah — beloved son bearing the means of his own death. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 22:6** — Isaac carries the wood. - **Isa 53:7** — Silent like a lamb. - **Num 19** — Heifer outside the camp. ## Shared structure - Beloved son carries wood - Father purposes offering - …

to.title: Garden Of Gethsemane As Eden Reversed
to.body: # Garden of Gethsemane as Eden Reversed ## NT Text — Matt 26:36-46; John 18:1 Jesus enters a garden to face the will of his Father. Where Adam in the garden chose his own will and brought death, the last Adam chooses the Father's will and accepts death to bring life. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 2-3** — Eden — the garden of disobedience. - **Gen 3:17-19** — Toil and sweat as curse. - **2 Sam 15:23** …

from.title: Absalom Hung On Tree
from.body: # Absalom Hangs in a Tree → Cursed One on the Tree ## OT Story — 2 Samuel 18:9-15 Absalom's hair catches in the branches of a great oak; he hangs between heaven and earth while his mule rides on. Joab pierces him with three darts. ## NT Fulfillment - Galatians 3:13: Cursed is everyone who hangs on a tree. - Deuteronomy 21:22-23: A hanged man is cursed by God. - John 19:34: Pierced in the side. ## …

to.title: Bathsheba Uriah Sin
to.body: # David's Sin with Bathsheba → Royal Failure and Atoning Need ## OT Story — 2 Samuel 11 King David, idle in spring war-season, takes Bathsheba; she conceives. To cover it, he orders Uriah's death in battle. The thing he had done displeased the LORD. ## NT Fulfillment - Psalm 51: David's penitential prayer. - 1 John 1:9: If we confess, he is faithful and just to forgive. - Matthew 1:6: Listed in ge…

from.title: Elijah Chariot Of Fire
from.body: # Elijah's Chariot of Fire ## What happens Elijah and Elisha cross the parted Jordan; a chariot of fire and horses of fire separate them, and Elijah ascends in a whirlwind to heaven. ## Typological significance Bodily translation without death — anticipates the Ascension of Christ and the eschatological rapture of the saints. The mantle passing to Elisha types the giving of the Spirit to successor…

to.title: Philip Translated
to.body: # Philip Translated to Azotus ## What happens Immediately after baptizing the Ethiopian eunuch, the Spirit of the Lord catches Philip away, and he is found at Azotus, preaching through the cities to Caesarea. ## Typological significance Elijah-like translation by the Spirit — the new prophetic age moves at the Spirit's speed. Baptism of an Ethiopian fulfills Isaiah and Psalm 68's vision of Cush st…

from.title: Jacobs Twelve Sons
from.body: # Jacob's Twelve Sons → The Twelve Tribes and the Twelve Apostles ## OT Story — Genesis 29-30; 49 Through Leah, Rachel, and their handmaids, Jacob fathers twelve sons. In Genesis 49 he blesses each, prophesying tribal destinies. ## NT Fulfillment - Matthew 19:28: The twelve will sit on twelve thrones judging the tribes. - Revelation 21:12-14: The New Jerusalem bears the names of the twelve tribes …

to.title: Reuben Defiled Bed
to.body: # Reuben Loses the Birthright → Firstborn Privilege Forfeited ## OT Story — Genesis 35:22; 49:3-4 Reuben, the natural firstborn, lies with his father's concubine. In Jacob's deathbed blessing he is told, 'Unstable as water, you shall not have preeminence.' Birthright passes to Joseph, kingship to Judah. ## NT Fulfillment - Hebrews 12:16-17: Like Esau, those who despise the holy lose the inheritanc…

from.title: Beating Swords Into Plowshares
from.body: # Beating Swords into Plowshares ## The oracle "They shall beat their swords into plowshares, and their spears into pruning hooks; nation shall not lift up sword against nation, neither shall they learn war anymore." ## Date / context Isaiah and Micah, late 8th c. BC. ## Fulfillment Awaits messianic consummation; partially realized wherever the gospel produces peace, and inverted by Joel 3:10 in j…

to.title: Stricken Shepherd
to.body: # Stricken Shepherd ## The oracle "Awake, O sword, against my shepherd, against the man who stands next to me… strike the shepherd, and the sheep will be scattered." ## Date / context Zechariah, post-exilic Jerusalem, late 6th c. BC. ## Fulfillment Jesus quotes this on the night of his arrest to predict the disciples' flight (Matthew 26:31; Mark 14:27). ## Connects to `[[../Titles/Good-Shepherd]]`…

from.title: Daniel 2 Stone Cut Without Hands
from.body: # Stone Cut Without Hands → Kingdom That Fills the Earth ## OT Story — Daniel 2:31-45 Nebuchadnezzar dreams of a colossus shattered by a stone cut without hands, which becomes a mountain filling the earth. The God of heaven sets up an everlasting kingdom. ## NT Fulfillment - Acts 4:11: The stone rejected. - Luke 20:17-18: The stone falls and crushes. - Matthew 13:31-32: Kingdom grows like a tree. …

to.title: Haggai Glory Of Latter House
to.body: # Glory of the Latter House → Christ Enters the Second Temple ## OT Story — Haggai 2:6-9 'Yet once more I will shake the heavens and the earth… and the desire of all nations shall come, and I will fill this house with glory… The glory of this latter house shall be greater than the former.' ## NT Fulfillment - Hebrews 12:26-29: Cited as future shaking, leaving only the unshakable. - Luke 21:5-6: Th…

from.title: Forty Years Wilderness
from.body: # Forty Years in Wilderness → Christ's Forty Days ## OT Story — Numbers 14:33-34 Israel is tested forty years in the wilderness — humbled, fed with manna, made to know that man does not live by bread alone (Deut 8:3). ## NT Fulfillment - Matthew 4:1-11: Jesus tested forty days in the wilderness — quotes Deuteronomy 8. - Hebrews 3:7-19: They could not enter because of unbelief. - Hebrews 4:1-11: A …

to.title: Water From Rock Meribah
to.body: # Rock at Meribah / Strike Twice → Christ Suffers Once ## OT Story — Numbers 20:1-13 God commands Moses to speak to the rock. Moses, in anger, strikes it twice. Water flows, but Moses is barred from the land for failing to honor God. ## NT Fulfillment - Hebrews 9:28: Christ was offered once to bear sins. - Hebrews 7:27: He did this once for all when he offered himself. - Romans 6:10: The death he …

from.title: Paul In Rome Unhindered
from.body: # Paul in Rome, Unhindered ## NT Text — Acts 28:30-31 Acts ends with Paul preaching the kingdom in Rome 'unhindered.' Jerusalem-to-Rome arc completes; the gospel reaches the ends of the earth. ## OT Antecedents - **Isa 49:6** — To the end of the earth. - **Ps 96:3** — Declare his glory among the nations. - **Dan 7:14** — All nations should serve him. ## Shared structure - Capital reached - Boldnes…

to.title: Pauls Defense Before Felix
to.body: # Paul's Defense Before Felix ## NT Text — Acts 24 Paul defends himself before Felix, speaking of righteousness, self-control, and the coming judgment. Felix is alarmed and dismisses him. ## OT Antecedents - **Dan 5** — Belshazzar trembles before the writing on the wall. - **Esth 6** — King unable to sleep before reversal. - **Ezek 33** — Watchman warning. ## Shared structure - Imprisoned witness …

from.title: Bitter Water Sweetened By Wood
from.body: # Bitter Waters of Marah Sweetened by Wood → Cross Sweetens Life ## OT Story — Exodus 15:22-27 Three days from the sea, Israel finds water but it is bitter. They cry; Moses throws a tree into the water and it becomes sweet. The LORD reveals himself as YHWH-Rapha, the healer. ## NT Fulfillment - 1 Peter 2:24: By his wounds you have been healed. - Galatians 3:13: Christ became a curse, hanging on a …

to.title: Crossing Red Sea Baptism
to.body: # Crossing the Red Sea → Baptism Into Moses, Baptism Into Christ ## OT Story — Exodus 14 Israel walks through the sea on dry ground; the waters return and drown Pharaoh's army. The old life of slavery is buried in the sea. ## NT Fulfillment - 1 Corinthians 10:1-2: 'All were baptized into Moses in the cloud and in the sea.' - Romans 6:3-4: Baptized into Christ's death, raised to walk in newness. - …

from.title: Golden Lampstand Spirit
from.body: # Golden Lampstand / Menorah → Sevenfold Spirit and the Churches ## OT Story — Exodus 25:31-40 A hammered-gold lampstand of seven branches, with almond-blossom cups, gives light in the holy place. Zechariah sees it again, fed by two olive trees. ## NT Fulfillment - Revelation 1:12-20: Seven golden lampstands are seven churches; one like a Son of Man walks among them. - Revelation 4:5: Seven torche…

to.title: Hanukkah Light Returns
to.body: # Hanukkah / Rededication of the Temple → Christ Speaks at the Feast ## OT Story — 1 Maccabees 4:36-59 After Antiochus' desecration, the Maccabees cleanse and rededicate the temple. The Feast of Dedication (Hanukkah) commemorates the return of light. ## NT Fulfillment - John 10:22-30: At the Feast of Dedication Jesus declares his unity with the Father. - John 8:12: I am the light of the world. - H…
